attrs: {'persona': 'a new parent', 'trigger': 'pressure at work or school', 'register': 'short and blunt', 'length': 'long'}
text:
u guys dont know wut its like to be a new parent rn. my tiny human is literally the only thing keeping me alive. but honestly, being a parent is exhausting. like, physically and mentally exhausting. i used to be able to do like 10 things at once without needing a nap, but now i can barely make it through the day without crashing.

my kid is only 6 months old, but i feel like i've lost myself in the process of raising them. i dont even know who i am no more. i used to love reading, hiking, painting, and just chillin. but now, the only thing i'm interested in is making sure my kid eats, sleeps, and poops. i mean, dont get me wrong, its amazing to see them grow, but its also super overwhelming.

i feel like im just going through the motions most days. i go to work, come home, feed the baby, put her down for a nap, and then watch tv until bedtime. its like i'm in a haze. i dont even know what i like or dislike anymore. i just know that i need to be doing something with my kid, and thats it.

my partner is always on my case about taking care of myself, but it feels like no matter how much sleep i get, no matter how much exercise i do, i just cant seem to shake this feeling of emptiness. i feel like im just a robot, just going through the motions until i can collapse onto the couch. and even then, i just feel so guilty for not being able to do more.

i just wish i could find my way back to myself again. i wish i could feel excited about things again. but for now, im just stuck in this rut, trying to figure out how to be a parent and still be a person.

attrs: {'persona': 'a middle-aged caregiver', 'trigger': 'social situations', 'register': 'lowercase, hurried, minimal punctuation', 'length': 'long'}
text:
i just got back from my sister's birthday party and i feel completely drained it was so loud and crowded and everyone was smiling and laughing but it all just felt so fake to me i couldn't muster up the energy to pretend to be happy or to pretend to be excited for her i just felt like a ghost wandering around the room not really part of it all i know i'm her sister and i should be happy for her but honestly i just can't fake it i feel like i'm just going through the motions of life and no one else really gets it 

i'm a caregiver and i've been taking care of my mom for years now and it's just so exhausting but no one ever asks me how i'm doing or if i need any help i just get told to be strong or to keep going and it feels like no one really cares about how i'm feeling i just feel so alone in this 

i try to put on a good face and pretend to be okay but inside i'm just crumbling i feel like i'm just existing rather than really living and it's so hard to explain to people that sometimes the best thing to do is just to sit in silence and not do anything at all 

i know i need to get out more and make an effort to socialize and meet new people but it's just so hard when all i want to do is curl up in bed and hide from the world i feel like i'm just a burden to everyone around me and it's so hard to shake that feeling 

i just wish people could understand that just because i'm happy all the time doesn't mean i'm not struggling and that just because i'm not asking for help doesn't mean i don't need it i just feel so invisible and unseen and it's so hard to find my voice

attrs: {'persona': 'a person living alone in a new city', 'trigger': 'an anniversary of a hard event', 'register': 'short and blunt', 'length': 'long'}
text:
can't believe its been a year since everything fell apart. i've been living in this new city for a few months now, but it feels like i'm still trying to find my place. i've got my routine, my apartment, but nothing really feels real.

today was my anniversary with her. we were together for 5 years, and it all just fell apart in one day. i thought we had it all figured out, but i guess i was wrong. she left, took our stuff, left me with this hole that i don't know how to fill.

i've been trying to stay busy, try to distract myself from the pain, but it's hard. everything reminds me of her. the park where we used to walk, the coffee shop where we'd meet for dates, even the songs we used to listen to together. it's all just a reminder of what i've lost.

i feel so empty, so lost. i don't know who i am anymore. i used to be this person, with this partner, with this life. now i'm just a shell of that person. i don't know how to move forward, how to find happiness again.

i've been trying to talk to people, make friends, but it's hard. everyone's got their own stuff, their own problems. no one really wants to listen to me, to hear about my pain. i feel like i'm just a burden, a reminder of someone else's happiness.

i guess i'm just feeling really stuck right now. i don't know how to get out of this rut, how to find my way again. i just feel so lost, so alone. i guess that's all anyone can expect from me right now. i'm just trying to make it through the day, one day at a time.

attrs: {'persona': 'a middle-aged caregiver', 'trigger': 'a health scare', 'register': 'short and blunt', 'length': 'long'}
text: can't shake this feeling of emptiness i've been taking care of my mom for years and now that she's finally getting the help she needs i'm left feeling lost and useless. it's not just the daily routine that's getting to me it's the thought of living without her here for me, for my family. we've all grown so accustomed to her presence it's like a part of us is missing. the doctor's orders are to take it easy and focus on myself but honestly i just feel like i'm just going through the motions. every day is a struggle to get out of bed, to get anything done. i feel like i'm just waiting for the other shoe to drop, for something else to happen. my mind is always racing with worst-case scenarios and my body aches from the weight of it all. i've tried to talk to my family about it but they just tell me to be strong and that i'm doing great. they don't understand. they don't know what it's like to be the one holding everything together all the time. i'm exhausted, physically and mentally. i feel like i'm drowning in a sea of responsibility and i don't know how to keep my head above water. sometimes i wonder if anyone would even notice if i just disappeared. would they even care? or would they just breathe a sigh of relief that they wouldn't have to deal with me anymore? i know that's not true but sometimes those thoughts creep in and they're hard to shake.

attrs: {'persona': 'a new parent', 'trigger': 'a health scare', 'register': 'hesitant, lots of qualifiers like "idk" and "maybe"', 'length': 'long'}
text:
I'm literally at my wit's end here. I just found out I've been pre-diagnosed with a condition that could affect my baby's health if I don't change my lifestyle immediately. I'm talking major lifestyle changes, like cutting back on caffeine, eating organic, all that jazz. 

I know some people have been through this and have come out the other side, but what if it's not that simple? What if it's just a never-ending cycle of feeling like crap? I'm exhausted all the time, idk why, but I am. I try to do stuff, like laundry or cooking, but it feels like such a chore. And the guilt, oh god, the guilt is overwhelming. 

I feel like I'm failing my baby in some way. Like, what if they get sick because of me? I've been so consumed by these thoughts, I haven't even left the house in days. My partner is trying to be supportive, but idk, it feels like they don't get it. They just keep saying "it's going to be okay" and "just do this one thing." But what's that one thing, you know?

I'm constantly questioning my own worth, wondering if I'm just not cut out for this parenting thing. Maybe I am just not meant to be a parent. Maybe I should just give up now and avoid any potential heartache. But then I look at my baby and I'm like, no, no, no, I'm not giving up. But what if it's too late? What if I've already messed up somehow?

I guess what I'm trying to say is, I feel like I'm stuck in this rut and I don't know how to get out. I'm not even sure if it's possible. I just feel so lost and alone.

attrs: {'persona': 'a night-shift worker', 'trigger': 'family conflict', 'register': 'short and blunt', 'length': 'long'}
text:
i cant even be bothered to write this right now i'm so tired all the time i feel like i'm just going through the motions of life and nothing matters anymore. been working night shifts for years and my body just cant keep up anymore. my back aches my eyes are always red my skin is dry and it feels like ive been living in a desert for months.

the worst part is my family is all over me about stuff i dont even care about. they keep asking me to come over and "dine" with them on saturdays and i just cant even. i dont even like eating anymore. i just put some leftovers in a microwave and call it a day. my mum keeps saying "just get some exercise and you'll feel better" like it's that simple. it's not. my body is broken and my mind is numb.

i try to tell them that but they just wont listen. they always just pat me on the head and say "oh, you're just having a bad day" or "it'll get better soon" like it's something i can just flip a switch and suddenly i'm fine. but i'm not fine. i'm not even close.

i just feel so empty and hollow all the time. i used to love my job as a mechanic but now even that feels like a chore. i just go through the motions and collect a paycheck. i dont even enjoy my own company anymore. its like i'm just living in a bubble and soon that bubble is gonna burst.

i dont know how much more of this i can take. i feel like i'm just pretending to be a person right now and not even sure why. maybe someday i'll find my way out of this hole but til then im stuck in this rut and i dont know how to climb out

text:

i try to talk to them, but idk, it's like they're not even listening. they just give me this patronizing speech about how i need to "toughen up" or "find my passion", but idk, it's not that easy. i'm just so... exhausted. all the time. i feel like i'm drowning in this sea of nothingness, and idk how to keep my head above water.

maybe i'm just being dramatic, but it feels like everyone's moving on with their lives, and i'm just stuck in this rut. idk, maybe i'm just not cut out for this whole "being an adult" thing. idk, maybe i'm just not good enough. but what's the point of even trying, right? i mean, i'm just gonna end up failing anyway.

attrs: {'persona': 'someone recently unemployed', 'trigger': 'an anniversary of a hard event', 'register': 'hesitant, lots of qualifiers like "idk" and "maybe"', 'length': 'long'}
text:
today's the anniversary of when i lost my job and my whole world fell apart. idk how to even write this post without feeling like a total failure. i've been thinking about it a lot lately and maybe that's what i need to do. try to process my emotions and not just suppress them like i always do.

i don't know if anyone else feels like this but i genuinely don't know how to get excited about stuff anymore. idk if it's the lack of purpose or just the constant stress of dealing with job applications but it feels like every day is just a repeat of the same monotonous cycle.

maybe it's because i'm not even supposed to be doing job applications anymore. idk why it feels like that's weighing me down more than the actual rejection letters or unreturned phone calls. maybe it's just my brain trying to find a way to make sense of things.

i do remember when i first got laid off, i was devastated. i felt like my whole identity was tied up in that job and suddenly i was just a nobody. idk how i coped with that at the time but now it just feels like a constant reminder of my worthlessness.

i know i shouldn't be feeling this way but idk, it feels like everyone around me is moving forward with their lives while i'm just stuck in neutral. i've been trying to get help but idk what the point is if i'm just going to end up feeling the same way.

i don't know if i'll ever be able to get out of this rut but maybe talking about it is the first step. idk, maybe that's why i'm still here today, hoping to find some kind of sense of purpose or meaning in my life again. idk if that's even possible for me right now but idk what else i can do.

attrs: {'persona': 'a person living alone in a new city', 'trigger': 'moving to a new place', 'register': 'short and blunt', 'length': 'long'}
text:
i've been here for like 3 months now and i'm still trying to figure things out but honestly it feels like i'm just drifting through life. every day is the same routine - wake up, go to my place, watch tv, go to work, come home, watch more tv. i don't really have any friends here and people at work are nice but we're all so busy that we barely talk. i try to make an effort to explore the city but i just end up feeling lost and alone. the places i go to seem empty and lifeless too. i went to this café the other day and it was one of those moments where i felt like i was the only person in the world. the barista was nice but it felt like we were just pretending to be friendly or something. after a few minutes of chatting, i just sat there in silence, staring at my phone, feeling like a complete outsider.

i've tried to shake off this feeling but it's hard to do when every day feels like a repetition. i don't really have any hobbies or interests here that i'm passionate about. i just kind of exist. sometimes i think about my old life and how it was more exciting, but that's just nostalgia. i know i'm not really missing anything. i'm just stuck in this rut and i don't know how to get out of it. i feel like i'm wasting my life away and it's hard to see a way out of this place. sometimes i just wish i could find something that makes me feel alive again, but so far nothing has.

text:
im just sitting here in my new apartment staring at the empty walls feeling so lost i dont know how i ended up here and why i thought moving to a new city was a good idea i mean its not like i hate my old city or anything but its not my hometown either so why did i want to leave? maybe its just the fear of routine and familiar faces and people who know me but now im just surrounded by strangers and im feeling so empty its like im drowning in my own thoughts

i went to the grocery store yesterday and saw this one friend from back home and we ran into each other by chance and i was so surprised i didnt even know how to react but as soon as she asked me how i was doing i just shut down and changed the subject and now im feeling guilty for not being happy for her and for lying to her but i dont know how to tell her the truth its not that i dont like her or anything its just that i dont know what to do with myself right now

i miss my old life and my old routine and my old friends and i miss the sense of security and familiarity that came with it but its like im trying to recreate everything from scratch and its just not working and im starting to feel like im not good enough or worthy of happiness or love or connection or anything anymore

i dont know what the point of all this is or what im trying to achieve but i feel like im just going through the motions of living right now and im not even sure if thats enough or if thats all i need to be happy or if thats just a myth thats been fed to me by society or by my parents or by god knows who but right now it just feels like theres no point to anything

text:
idk how much more of this i can take. family's been on my case about something stupid, and i'm just so exhausted. like, i get it, they're just worried, but idk why they have to be so extra. always on my case about something. maybe if i just tried harder, they'd leave me alone. but honestly, idk if it's worth it. every time we talk about it, i just feel like i'm failing them or something.

i don't know what's wrong with me. i used to love my job, i used to love talking to people, but now it just feels like so much work. and even when i try to talk to them about it, they just get defensive. like, i'm the one who's not making an effort. idk, maybe i am just not cut out for this whole adulting thing. maybe i'm just too sensitive.

i feel like i'm just going through the motions every day. wake up, go to work, come home, repeat. i don't know what i want to do with my life anymore. maybe i'm just too burnt out to care. idk. all i know is that i feel really empty inside, like there's this huge hole in my chest that nothing can fill. and no matter what i do, it just feels like it's getting bigger and bigger.

i know i need to talk to someone about this, but idk who to turn to. my family's not exactly the most supportive bunch, and my friends are all busy with their own stuff. maybe i just need to take some time for myself, figure some things out. but idk, that sounds like even more work than just doing what i'm already doing.

text:
im 3 days away from exam and im just so done with everything. school work, assignments, studying... i used to enjoy learning and all that, but now it just feels like a chore. i get up every morning feeling empty, like i have no energy or motivation to do anything. i just wanna crawl back into bed and sleep the day away.

i know my friends and family are counting on me to do well, but honestly, what's the point? i cant even remember what i wanted to study in the first place, and now it feels like im just going through the motions. i try to focus on the positive, but it's hard when every time i think about the exam, i feel a knot in my stomach.

i hate how school has become this constant source of stress and anxiety in my life. i used to be able to just chill and relax, but now it feels like im constantly worrying about something. i just want to feel normal again, like im not some broken machine that cant function without a certain level of stress.

i know im not alone in this, but it feels like nobody gets it. everyone says "just study harder" or "you got this", but nobody actually understands what its like to feel this way. i feel like im just pretending to be fine when im really just a mess.

im not asking for sympathy or anything, i just wish somebody could understand that i'm not just being lazy or unmotivated. i'm just exhausted, plain and simple. is that too much to ask?

attrs: {'persona': 'a university student in exam season', 'trigger': 'an anniversary of a hard event', 'register': 'short and blunt', 'length': 'long'}
text:
today's the anniversary of my first year of uni and i'm still stuck in the same old routine. same old lectures, same old people, same old everything. it feels like im just going through the motions. i have so many unfulfilled promises to myself, so many goals i want to achieve but its hard to see a way out of this tunnel.

i keep replaying the same conversation in my head. the one where my mum told me i could do anything i set my mind to. but then i see the news about all these talented artists struggling to make ends meet. it makes me wonder if i'm just deluding myself. if i'm just pretending to be something im not.

i used to love my major, i really did. i used to get so excited about learning new things. but now its all just so...pointless. i feel like im just wasting my time, wasting my potential. and the worst part is, im not even sure whats holding me back. is it the fear of failure? the fear of success? i dont know anymore.

i look around at my friends and they all seem so much more motivated. they all seem to have their lives together. but i know they dont. i see the cracks in their facades, the desperation in their eyes. and its making me feel like im the only one who really gets it. like im the only one who understands that nobody has it all together.

i dont know how to break this cycle. i dont know how to get out of this rut. all i know is that i feel so empty, so exhausted. i feel like im just going through the motions. and the thought of doing it all again tomorrow is just too much to handle.

text:
idk if i'm even doing this right but i need to talk to someone about how i'm feeling lately. i'm not sure what's wrong with me or if anyone would even care, but i just feel really empty all the time. like, my family's always asking me how my day was and i'm just like "idk" and they're all "oh, okay, well, can you help us with dinner?" and i'm just sitting there thinking "no, i can barely take care of myself right now". maybe i'm just burnt out or maybe it's something more serious, but idk.

i used to love my job, i really did. i used to get so excited for work every morning, but now just getting out of bed feels like too much effort. and even when i'm at work, i just feel so disconnected from everyone and everything. i'm just going through the motions, you know? i'm not really living anymore. my friends are all getting married, having kids, and starting their careers, and i'm just... here. feeling like a failure or something.

i don't know what the solution is, though. maybe i just need to make some changes, like finding a new job or something, but idk. i'm scared of change, i guess. what if it makes things worse? what if i'm just not cut out for this adult thing? idk, maybe i'm just being lazy or something, but it feels like no one would even notice if i just vanished. and that thought is kinda sad, i guess. i feel like i'm just a ghost haunting my own life, invisible and unimportant.

okay so its been like 3 months since i lost my job and things have been getting really rough financially i mean i still have a place to live and food on the table but just barely i feel like im drowning in debt and i dont know how im supposed to get out of this mess i apply to a million jobs a day but no one wants to hire anyone with 'unstable' income its like i am a black hole to potential employers

i keep thinking about how far i've fallen from where i was like 5 years ago when i was on top of the world with my job and money and friends everything was going great and now look at me i cant even afford to take a weekend trip to see my family its like i've lost everything

i feel like im just going through the motions of life right now like im in a never-ending nightmare and im just waiting for it to be over i dont have any motivation to do anything or make any changes i just exist and thats it

i try to remind myself that things will get better but it feels like that light at the end of the tunnel is getting fainter and fainter and i just want to crawl under a blanket and hide from the world until it goes away but i know thats not an option because i have bills to pay and responsibilities to fulfill

i just wish i could get a grip on my life and take control of my finances but it feels like every step forward is met with 10 steps back i just feel so lost and alone and like im just drifting through life without any direction or purpose

attrs: {'persona': 'a night-shift worker', 'trigger': 'moving to a new place', 'register': 'short and blunt', 'length': 'long'}
text:
i've been working nights for years now, and i thought i was used to it. but moving to a new place has messed with my head. every night feels like a never ending shift, and i'm so tired all the time. i wake up at 7, go to bed at 3... and still feel like crap. my days are just a blur of unpacking, cleaning, trying to make a new life here. but nothing feels right.

i try to go out, but everything just feels so empty. i see my friends, we go to bars, try to have fun... but it's all just a chore. i'm not even hungry, i just go through the motions. and when we're at home, i just want to be alone. i don't want to talk, i don't want to do anything. i just want to curl up on the couch and forget about the whole thing.

my job is the same, just different hours. i'm tired all the time, my focus is shot... i just feel like i'm going through the motions. i've been trying to talk to my coworkers about it, but no one seems to get it. they're all just like "oh, you're just tired, it'll pass". but it won't. it's not just tired. it's like... i just don't care anymore.

i miss my old place, i miss my old routine. i miss being able to just go to bed at 10 and wake up at 6. now it's all about making a new life, and it's just so exhausting. i feel like i'm losing myself in all of this. i don't know how to get out of this rut. i just feel so empty, so drained. i don't know how much more of this i can take.

text:

attrs: {'persona': 'a new parent', 'trigger': 'family conflict', 'register': 'hesitant, lots of qualifiers like "idk" and "maybe"', 'length': 'long'}
text:
idk how much more of this i can take. i feel like i'm just pretending to be a good parent all the time. maybe im just really bad at it. everyone says i should be confident and not let anyone bring me down, but honestly idk how to even try sometimes. my kid is literally the best thing that's ever happened to me, but that doesn't mean i have to be happy all the time. maybe that's just unrealistic.

i'm constantly worried about what my kid is doing at school. maybe they're getting bullied or left behind. or maybe i'm just being a helicopter parent and i'm actually doing the right thing. idk. all i know is that i feel guilty when i'm not there for them and my partner is. but what if i'm just not cut out for this parenting thing?

i look around at other parents and they all seem so... put together. confident. like they have it all figured out. and i'm just over here, pretending to be one of them. i'm the one who has to deal with the messes my kid makes, who has to make the hard decisions about their education and their healthcare. maybe that's just my lot in life.

sometimes i feel like i'm just going through the motions. like i'm just a robot, pretending to be a parent. idk how to shake this feeling. maybe i need to just take a step back and try to find my own identity outside of motherhood. but idk if i can do that. idk if i'll ever be able to balance my own needs with being a good parent. idk

text: oh man i swear i've been cooking so much lately its been a week i think since i last had time to myself and i know its not ideal but you know how its been weve been short staffed here at the nursing home and i just cant say no to the girls theyre counting on me so i was out in the kitchen making lunch for the residents and i saw this recipe for lemon chicken that i just had to try and lets just say it was a hit the way the flavors all came together its like a symphony or something anyway i was chopping up the herbs and i realized id forgotten to buy fresh parsley and i swear i wouldve been so annoyed if i had to go out and get some because i was already running behind schedule so i ended up using dried parsley instead and it was still pretty good i mean its not the same but its close enough and the residents seemed to like it too when i served it up to them anyway enough about cooking what ive been trying to get back into is my regular workouts too i used to be able to run 3 times a week no problem but since having kids it feels like ive lost all my momentum and i just cant seem to get motivated to get out the door its funny how our priorities change when we have little ones at home they need so much attention and care and its hard to just leave them to fend for themselves even for a few hours a week so i started just taking short walks around the block with the baby in the stroller and its been great for me and the baby its like we have our own little routine going on and its nice to get some fresh air and just clear my head you know so thats what Ive been up to lately just trying to find a balance between work and family and trying to get myself back into shape again its been a challenge but im determined to get it done and i feel like i have it under control for the most part anyway thats my update on life just the usual stuff its not exciting but its real life and thats what makes it so worth it

so i'm trying to start this little project in my free time and i don't wanna jinx it or anything but maybe i'll finally get around to making a photo album for my niece's birthday, which is like, next week or something idk if i have all the supplies and pics i need but i'll figure it out as i go, right? i mean, it's just gonna be a bunch of scraps and mementos from our hangouts and stuff so it's not like it's gonna be some perfect, curated masterpiece or anything maybe just a messy, heartfelt mess.

text:
idk if anyone else has ever felt like this but like i just got back from work and i'm just so exhausted but not in a bad way? i mean, i feel like i'm finally starting to get the hang of this whole parenthood thing and it's just really sweet. my baby girl is 8 weeks old and it's crazy how fast she's growing up already. yesterday i was trying to get her to sleep and i was just so frustrated that i couldn't get her to calm down, but today i felt like i was just able to soothe her with like, 2.5 minutes of rocking instead of 10. maybe it's just because i'm getting better at this or maybe it's because she's just really chill today. idk.

anyway, it's days like today that make me think i'm actually doing something right. like, i was at work and my coworker was like "oh my god, how are you doing that?" and i was like "um, idk, maybe i just haven't taken a nap in 3 days?" and they were all like "no, seriously, you're doing amazing!" and i'm just like "okay, maybe i am doing okay". it's funny because sometimes i feel like i'm just winging it and that my baby is going to end up with some major issues because of it, but today i just felt like i was in sync with her, you know?

i guess what i'm saying is that it's just really nice to feel like i'm doing something right, even if it's just a small thing like getting her to sleep for 30 minutes without crying. i mean, it's not like i'm some kind of superhero or anything, but it's just... it's nice. maybe i'll try to hang onto this feeling for a while, because it's definitely a nice distraction from the constant questions of "are you okay?". idk, maybe i am okay

text:
idk how i'm doing this but i feel like i'm stuck in this never-ending cycle of highs and lows and i don't know how to get out. money's been tight for like ever and it's been affecting me in ways i never thought it would. i was talking to my therapist about it and she said maybe it's a symptom of something deeper but i don't know what to think. do i just get anxious about money because i'm anxious about everything? maybe i'm just looking for external validation in the form of a steady paycheck.

i've been trying to focus on my grades and all that but honestly it feels like i'm just pretending to be someone i'm not. i'm like a chameleon or something. one day i'm all about my schoolwork, the next i'm hanging out with my friends and pretending to care about their drama. maybe i'm just afraid of being vulnerable because if i let my guard down, what if they leave me too?

i had a friend over last night and we were just talking about our problems and it was so easy to get caught up in the conversation. we were both just being super honest and it felt like we really understood each other. but then she said something and it completely flipped the script. she mentioned that she's been seeing someone else and i was like totally blindsided. i don't know why but it felt like a punch to the gut.

i guess what i'm trying to say is that i feel like i'm constantly playing a role and it's exhausting. i don't know who i am or what i want or even if i want anything. maybe that's just the nature of it all, but it feels like i'm losing myself in the process. i don't know how to find my way back to... to me, i guess.

attrs: {'persona': 'a person living alone in a new city', 'trigger': 'an anniversary of a hard event', 'register': 'long and rambling with run-on sentences', 'length': 'long'}
text:
today marks another year since they left. the city feels empty, a hollow monument to our time together. i wander these streets, feeling like a ghost, invisible and insignificant. everyone else is moving, their lives unfolding like a map, while mine remains stuck in the static image of what we had.

i thought i was ready, that the pain had dulled enough for me to begin again. but the memories still linger, a perpetual ache in my chest. it's like trying to hold onto a flame - the closer i get, the more it slips through my fingers. they say time heals all wounds, but what about the ones that refuse to scar?

i've been trying to fill the void with people, with moments, but nothing sticks. i'm a chameleon, adapting to the colors of the crowd, but it's all just a facade. behind the mask, i'm still that same lost soul, searching for a connection that never was.

i've been thinking about what we had, about the laughter, the tears, the moments of tenderness. it's all still so vivid, like a movie that refuses to end. i replay those conversations, wondering what i could've said, what i could've done differently. the what-ifs consume me, a never-ending cycle of regret.

i've tried to move on, to find solace in the anonymity of this new city. but it's like trying to find a new language - the words feel foreign, the rhythm off. everyone looks like they're living, but i'm just going through the motions. i'm a stranger in my own skin, a stranger in this city. and on anniversaries like today, the pain feels like it's all coming back, like i'm reliving the hurt all over again.

attrs: {'persona': 'a recent graduate living back home', 'trigger': 'an upcoming deadline or exam', 'register': 'long and rambling with run-on sentences', 'length': 'medium'}
text:
i'm drowning in a sea of questions as i stare blankly at the clock on my wall, the countdown to my exam ticking away like a metronome in my mind. i'm supposed to be in a place of clarity and focus, but all i can think about is how everyone around me seems to have their lives together except me. my mom's all like 'you're so talented and smart, just calm down and trust the process', but her words ring hollow when i look around at everyone else's highlight reels on social media.

i feel like i'm just pretending to be someone i'm not, a fragile mask that slips and reveals the truth - that i'm just a lost soul trying to make sense of it all. and then there's the fear that grips me like a vice whenever i think about what will happen when the semester ends and i have to leave this place again. will they all just... forget about me? or worse, leave me behind like the fragile shell of a snail that i am. all i want is to be seen, to be heard, to be more than just a footnote in the grand narrative of my family's life.

attrs: {'persona': 'a young professional', 'trigger': 'sleep problems', 'register': 'short and blunt', 'length': 'medium'}
text:
i've been lying awake for hours, staring at the ceiling, and wondering why i'm even here. the city outside my window never sleeps, but to me, it might as well. i feel like a ghost haunting my own life.

tonight was supposed to be a big meeting at work. i was supposed to nail it. but now, as the minutes tick by, i'm starting to wonder if i even belong. do i really have anything to offer? or am i just a placeholder, a temporary fix?

i keep thinking about all the people who've left me in the past. my ex, my friends from childhood... they all seem to have moved on with their lives, leaving me behind. and yet, i still find myself idealizing them, remembering the way they used to make me feel. like i'm just a canvas, waiting for the next brushstroke to erase my existence.

i'm so tired of being invisible. tired of being replaceable. tired of being alone.

attrs: {'persona': 'a middle-aged caregiver', 'trigger': 'moving to a new place', 'register': 'hesitant, lots of qualifiers like "idk" and "maybe"', 'length': 'long'}
text:
i dont know what im doing here or who im supposed to be but the address says my name on it. moving to a new place is always weird, but this time feels especially strange. i keep thinking i'm just pretending to be someone else.

idk if thats just the stress of the past few weeks or what, but i feel like im just going through the motions. making friends is easy enough, but it feels forced, like im putting on a mask. people seem so real here, so genuine, and it freaks me out a little bit.

i keep thinking about all the people im leaving behind, the ones i thought i was doing okay for. but maybe i was just pretending, playing a role. do i really know anyone, or am i just a stranger in a new city, trying to fit in?

my sister keeps asking me if im okay, but idk what that even means. am i? am i supposed to be happy, or is that just what people want me to be? maybe i should just be honest with myself for once, but what if thats just another lie?

i got a weird feeling when i was unpacking boxes today - like something was watching me, following me. i told myself it was just my imagination, but the feeling lingers. maybe im just paranoid, or maybe im just scared of being alone.

i dont know who i am or what i want, but i do know i need to get out of here. at least for a little while. the city feels so big and overwhelming, but maybe thats just what i need. a change of scenery, a fresh start. or maybe im just running from my problems, from myself.

i dont know. idk. maybe ill just figure it out as i go. thats what everyone says, right? maybe i should just take their word for it.

attrs: {'persona': 'a recent graduate living back home', 'trigger': 'an anniversary of a hard event', 'register': 'short and blunt', 'length': 'medium'}
text:
today's the anniversary of the summer that shattered me. i was 20, invincible. now i'm 22, still trying to pick up the pieces. 
every year i thought i'd get over it, but it's like my brain has a permanent reset button. i replay the same fragmented memories, wondering what i did wrong. 
i see my family's old photos, still with me, but i'm not in them. my friends, gone. 
today, i felt like i was living in someone else's skin. like i was just going through motions. i pretended to be the person i used to be, the one who was supposed to be happy, but it's just a lie. 
i asked my mom for a hug, but she just smiled and said 'you're so strong'. strong. yeah, right. i'm just pretending to be strong, just like i pretend to be the person i used to be. but inside, i'm still 20, screaming at myself to wake up. to stop running. but the running never stops.

attrs: {'persona': 'a middle-aged caregiver', 'trigger': 'moving to a new place', 'register': 'long and rambling with run-on sentences', 'length': 'long'}
text:
oh god the house is finally sold i'm moving to a new place for the kids and my husband but for me it feels like an escape from myself from the expectations and the memories that linger everywhere we're supposed to start fresh but what does that even mean for me how do i leave behind the person i used to be the one i thought i was supposed to be

i keep thinking about all the faces i'll see here new faces new people new relationships but what if they can't see me what if they see the cracks in the facade the mask i've worn for so long the one that says i'm a good mother a good wife a good person but what if they see the emptiness beneath

i feel like i'm being pulled in a million directions trying to be the perfect caregiver trying to be the perfect partner trying to be the perfect person but it's all just a juggling act and the balls are always about to drop and when they do i'll be left with nothing but the feeling of weightlessness

my husband keeps telling me to take a deep breath to focus on the positive but what's positive about leaving behind the only home i've ever known the only place i've ever felt like i fit in the only place i've ever felt like i was a part of something bigger than myself

i'm scared of being alone scared of being lost scared of being left behind but at the same time i'm scared of being seen scared of being exposed scared of being found wanting but what if that's exactly what i need i what if this new place is exactly what i need to lose myself and find myself all over again

i don't know what the future holds or what the present will bring but i do know that i have to keep moving forward even if it kills me even if it destroys me even if it leaves me with nothing but the echoes of my own uncertainty

attrs: {'persona': 'a night-shift worker', 'trigger': 'a relationship breakup', 'register': 'hesitant, lots of qualifiers like "idk" and "maybe"', 'length': 'long'}
text:
i dont even know how to process this yet idk i thought we had something real, but i guess i was just deluding myself. maybe we were just two people trying to fill the voids in our lives. maybe we were just two strangers who found each other for a hot second and now its over.

i keep thinking about all the nights i spent driving home from work, feeling so alone and scared, and then you'd be there, smiling at me, telling me everything's gonna be alright. maybe you were just being nice, maybe you didn't really care. idk, maybe i was just so desperate for someone to care that i misread the whole thing.

i feel like im losing my grip on reality or something. one minute we were inseparable, the next minute youre telling me you're not good for me and you need space and i'm just sitting there, trying to make sense of it all. maybe i'm just not good enough, maybe i'm just not worth saving.

i keep going back to our last fight, trying to relive it, replay it in my head, see if there was something i could've done differently. maybe i should've just stayed silent, maybe i should've just walked away. but what if i did that? what if i just gave up and let you go?

i dont even know who i am without you. am i even a person? am i just a shell of a person, a hollow shell of someone who used to exist. idk, maybe i'm just overreacting, maybe this is all just some stupid breakup drama. but the emptiness is suffocating me, it's like theres this huge hole in my chest and i dont know how to fill it. maybe i just need time to heal, maybe i need to find myself again. but for now, i'm just lost, and scared, and alone.

attrs: {'persona': 'a new parent', 'trigger': 'social situations', 'register': 'hesitant, lots of qualifiers like "idk" and "maybe"', 'length': 'long'}
text:
u guys idk if im just being super paranoid rn, but every time my partner leaves the apartment to run errands, i feel this suffocating emptiness - like i'm literally drowning in my own skin. i try to shake it off, tell myself idk what i'd do without them, that i'm fine, but those thoughts keep creeping back in.

maybe it's the way they look at me, like i'm this fragile little thing that needs constant validation. i'm not fragile, though - i'm strong, idk, capable of so much more than i realize. but what if they're just humoring me? what if they're already planning to leave and i'm just being stupid?

i went out with friends last night, and it felt so forced. everyone was talking about this new restaurant, and i'm just sitting there, idk what to say, feeling like an outsider. my friends all keep asking me when i'm gonna settle down, get a stable job, and i'm just idk, maybe im not cut out for this. maybe im just a hot mess.

and don't even get me started on relationships. i had this one guy, a few years ago... we were perfect, or so i thought. but then he just disappeared, left me with nothing but a bunch of unanswered texts and a shattered heart. maybe i'm just too much to handle. maybe i'm some kind of toxic vortex that sucks everyone in and spits them out.

i feel like i'm constantly switching between these two personas - the weak, needy one, and the strong, independent one. idk which one is the real me, or if i even have a real me. all i know is that i'm scared, and i don't know how to make sense of it all. maybe i just need to ride out this storm and hope that the sun comes out again someday.

attrs: {'persona': 'a young professional', 'trigger': 'social situations', 'register': 'short and blunt', 'length': 'long'}
text:
why do i always end up being the one who needs to 'save' everyone else, but somehow i'm the one who's always on the verge of collapse? i'm the best listener, the most empathetic friend, the most loyal partner - but do anyone else notice how i'm always the one who's 'on edge', who's always the one who's 'crashing'?

at the party last night, everyone was oohing and ahhing over my 'crazy' stories and 'dramatic' antics, but when the music started, i slipped away unnoticed, disappearing into the crowd. i'm not even sure why i do that - is it to recharge, to escape the crushing weight of everyone else's expectations? or is it just a desperate attempt to prove to myself that i still exist?

i used to love being the center of attention, the life of the party, the one who brings everyone together. but now, it feels like i'm just a prop, a tool for everyone else to use and discard. and the worst part is, i'm the one who's always the first to realize when it's time to leave, when the conversation is going stale, when the air is growing thick with unspoken resentment.

i catch myself idealizing people, people who barely know me, just so i can feel seen and heard for once. and then, just as quickly, i'm discarding them, pushing them away, convinced that they're just using me for my 'interesting' stories or my 'emotional depth'. it's like i'm addicted to this toxic cycle, this masochistic dance of being desired and rejected in equal measure.

sometimes i wonder if i'm even worthy of love and connection - or if i'm just a perpetual outsider, a ghost hovering on the periphery of everyone's lives.

attrs: {'persona': 'a middle-aged caregiver', 'trigger': 'social situations', 'register': 'long and rambling with run-on sentences', 'length': 'long'}
text:
can't take it anymore, feeling like a chameleon in every social situation. one minute i'm the calm, caring wife, the next i'm the anxious, clingy best friend. people always seem to appreciate these different facets of me, but what about the space in between? that's where the suffocating dread seeps in.

at a dinner party last night, i found myself oscillating between being the witty conversationalist and the anxious introvert. my partner and i were chatting about our shared childhood memories, and then the conversation shifted to their new job, and before i knew it, i was questioning my own life choices. why do i always end up being the listener, while they're the ones basking in the spotlight?

i try to convince myself it's just nerves, but the mirror reflects a more sinister truth. i'm terrified of being abandoned, of being left behind like a forgotten glove. so i overcompensate by being overly attentive, pouring all my energy into the people around me, only to watch them grow distant and disinterested. it's a toxic cycle, but i don't know how to break free.

and then there are the relationships that start off with such fervor and idealization, only to fizzle out in a blaze of indifference. my heart is a war zone, with love and hate fighting for dominance. i've lost count of the number of times i've been left heartbroken and wondering what went wrong. was it something i did? something i didn't do? the questions plague me, keeping me up at night, as i scroll through the faces of my acquaintances, searching for validation.

i'm a puzzle with missing pieces, a canvas without a brush. my sense of self is a fleeting mirage, a will-o'-the-wisp that vanishes when i try to grasp it. sometimes i wonder if it's even worth trying to find my way, or if i'll just end up lost in the labyrinth of my own making.

attrs: {'persona': 'a new parent', 'trigger': 'a relationship breakup', 'register': 'short and blunt', 'length': 'long'}
text:
i'm supposed to be a mother now, but who am i even supposed to be? one minute i was this perfect, put together person, and the next i was just a mess, a shell of a person my ex left behind. he said i was too clingy, too needy, that i suffocated him. but what does that even mean? is it true? i feel like i'm just floating in a sea of uncertainty.

i remember when we were together, i thought i knew him so well. i thought i knew myself. but now that he's gone, i realize i didn't know anything. everything was a lie. the way he talked about me to his friends, the way he made me feel in front of his family... it was all just a show. a performance.

i'm scared that i'll never be able to escape this. that i'll be stuck in this rut forever, with no direction, no purpose. that i'll just be another forgotten, abandoned soul, lost in the vastness of this world. i'm scared of being alone, but at the same time, i'm scared of being with someone else. what if they leave me too? what if they think i'm too much to handle?

i look at my baby, sleeping peacefully in her crib, and i feel a pang of desperation. i want to be a good mother, to be a good person. but how do i even begin? i feel like i'm staring into the abyss, with no safety net to catch me if i fall. i just want to hold on to something, anything, but it feels like it's slipping through my fingers like sand.

i keep thinking about all the people i've loved and lost, all the people i've hurt and abandoned. is this my pattern? is this how i'm supposed to live my life? forever torn between love and despair, forever chasing the highs and the lows? i don't know, but i do know that i'm scared, and that's all that matters right now.

text:
idk how much more of this i can take. i feel like i'm just going through the motions, pretending to be a functioning adult, but secretly i'm just lost in the wilderness of my own mind. i keep thinking about that conversation with my best friend, and how i totally overreacted. i mean, maybe i did, but her words really hit a nerve. it's like, i know i'm not the only one with financial struggles, but it feels so isolating.

anyway, i woke up this morning and just couldn't shake the feeling of emptiness. like, i have a job, a place to live, but what's the point? i feel so unfulfilled and disconnected from everything. maybe it's because i'm just so worried about money all the time. i know i should be grateful for what i have, but it's hard not to compare myself to others.

i tried to reach out to my coworker, jenny, and just ended up getting defensive and stuff. i don't know, maybe i'm just having a bad day. but honestly, i think i'm just scared of being rejected or abandoned again. like, what if i'm not enough? what if i'm just pretending to be someone i'm not? the thought of being alone, of not having anyone to rely on, it's just so terrifying.

i guess what i'm trying to say is that i feel like i'm stuck in this limbo, and i don't know how to get out. maybe i'm just not cut out for this whole adult thing. i feel like i'm just faking it, and that anyone can see right through me. i don't know, maybe i'm just being paranoid. but it's hard not to wonder, when every interaction feels like a test, and every decision feels like a risk.

attrs: {'persona': 'a new parent', 'trigger': 'sleep problems', 'register': 'short and blunt', 'length': 'long'}
text:
i used to be so perfect at being a mom. i mean, my baby's first smile was worth a lifetime. but now i'm stuck in this haze of exhaustion. sleep is just a distant memory, a myth i tell myself to calm down. my baby doesn't even care, does she? she just cries until i surrender, and then she's fine. i'm not even mad anymore, i'm just... lost.

i feel like i'm drowning in my own guilt. i'm a terrible mom, that's what i keep telling myself. i'm not good enough, not smart enough, not patient enough. the list goes on. and then my baby goes to grandma's house and suddenly i'm a functioning member of society again. grandma's house is like a magical bubble where i don't have to worry about anything. no dirty diapers, no midnight feedings, no endless worrying. but when my baby comes back to me, i'm right back where i started.

i hate how i do this. i idealize grandma, I guess you could say. she's perfect, patient, and loving. and then my baby gets back to me and i'm just a mess. a hot mess. i try to be better, i really do. but it's hard when you're so tired all the time. and your baby is so tiny and dependent on you. i feel like i'm constantly juggling a million balls in the air, and if one of them drops, everything comes crashing down.

sometimes i wonder if i'm just pretending to be a mom. if i'm just pretending to care. am i just going through the motions? i know that can't be it, because my baby loves me. doesn't she? or does she just love the idea of me? i don't know anymore. all i know is that i'm tired, and scared, and alone. and that's not okay. but what's okay? i don't know anymore.

attrs: {'persona': 'a young professional', 'trigger': 'a relationship breakup', 'register': 'long and rambling with run-on sentences', 'length': 'medium'}
text:
i just got out of the worst relationship of my life and i'm still trying to pick up the shattered remains of my ego. or at least, what's left of it. i swear, i thought we had it all - the long walks on the beach, the candlelit dinners, the whispers of forever in the dead of night. but no, i guess that was all just a clever ruse to keep me hooked on the highs of being with someone who only loved me for my own validation.

now that it's over, i'm left to stare at this hollow shell of a person i've become. the one where i'm simultaneously a successful marketing manager by day and a broken, vulnerable mess by night. i'm terrified of being alone, but at the same time, i'm not sure i can handle the chaos that comes with trying to navigate my feelings for someone new. my friends keep telling me to focus on myself, but how do you do that when every waking moment feels like a struggle to stay afloat in a sea of uncertainty? i guess that's just the thing about being me - i'm a master of juggling multiple personas, but at what cost?

attrs: {'persona': 'someone recently unemployed', 'trigger': 'no single clear trigger', 'register': 'short and blunt', 'length': 'medium'}
text:
i've been working on rebuilding my identity for what feels like an eternity, and honestly i'm still not sure if it's mine. every 2 weeks is a new beginning - a fresh start, new resume, new networking connections. but the moment i get close to someone, they inevitably ghost me or cut me off. i've been in 5 relationships this year alone and every single one ended with me being the one who 'moved on' or 'found someone better'.

the thing is, i love the thrill of the chase, the rush of adrenaline when i'm chasing after someone. but at the same time, the fear of being left behind is suffocating me. i try to convince myself i'm over it, that i deserve better, but the truth is i'm just as scared of being alone as i am of being hurt. i feel like i'm stuck in this never-ending cycle of trying to prove myself to others, and it's exhausting. is this who i am? or am i just a shell of who i used to be?

attrs: {'persona': 'a young professional', 'trigger': 'moving to a new place', 'register': 'lowercase, hurried, minimal punctuation', 'length': 'long'}
text:
i've been in this new city for a week now and already it feels like a betrayal. everyone says hello and smile and it's all so... fake. like they're just pretending to be my friends until they get bored or need something from me. i try to remember the names of the people i've met but they're all starting to blend together - faces in a crowd, voices in a sea of strangers. i'm starting to wonder if i'm just a small part of someone else's life or if i'm just standing in the shadows, invisible.

i keep thinking about jess and how i was all over her for weeks after we broke up. i'd show up at her work unannounced, just to see her smile, and then a week later i'd be ghosted and she'd never hear from me again. it's like i was just a phase for her, a fleeting infatuation. and now i'm here, in this new place, and everyone is just waiting for me to lose my grip or mess up so they can swoop in and "save me". i hate the way it feels, like i'm just a puzzle piece that everyone is waiting for to fit in.

sometimes i feel like i'm trapped in this never-ending cycle of loving and losing, of being the center of someone's universe for a hot second and then being discarded like trash. and the worst part is, i don't even remember what it's like to feel whole. to be someone who is just... okay with who they are. i'm stuck in this abyss, constantly searching for that elusive center, and it's exhausting.

i know i need to focus on myself and make new friends, but it's hard when every interaction feels like a test, like i'm waiting for the other shoe to drop. i just want to find someone who doesn't see me as a temporary high or a temporary low, but it feels like everyone is just waiting for the perfect moment to abandon me

attrs: {'persona': 'a new parent', 'trigger': 'a health scare', 'register': 'lowercase, hurried, minimal punctuation', 'length': 'long'}
text:
i just found out my daughter had a pretty severe allergic reaction to something in her food and i'm still trying to process the reality of it all. i keep thinking about how i wasn't even there for her when she was born and now she's almost a year old and we have to live with this constant fear of her health falling apart.

every time i look at her tiny face, i feel this overwhelming urge to protect her from everything - from the sun to the other kids at school. i want to shield her from every single thing that could possibly hurt her. but at the same time, the thought of not being able to shield her from her own fears and anxieties - i mean, i have anxiety too, you know - it's like my heart is just completely unraveling.

i try to remember when i felt this way about someone, someone who was supposed to be able to take care of me - my ex, i guess. i idealized him at first, i mean, we were in love and he was my whole world, but then he started to flake out and leave me hanging, and i was left to pick up the pieces of my shattered ego. i thought i'd gotten past that, that i'd grown and learned from it, but now that i'm holding my own daughter's life in my hands - i don't know if i can handle the pain of it all.

i don't know, maybe i'm just being paranoid or overly dramatic, but every time i look at her, i feel like she's this tiny, vulnerable thing that's completely dependent on me to keep her safe. and the thought of not being able to do that, of failing her in any way, it's like this crushing weight is pressing down on me, suffocating me.

i guess what i'm trying to say is, i feel so lost and so scared and so unsure of myself right now. i don't know if i'm good enough for this, if i'm strong enough to be her rock. but at the same time, i know i have to try, for her sake, not just mine.

attrs: {'persona': 'a young professional', 'trigger': 'social situations', 'register': 'short and blunt', 'length': 'long'}
text:
tonight was one of those nights where i felt like a chameleon, adapting to the environment around me but somehow losing myself in the process. at the dinner party, everyone seemed to be getting along swimmingly, except for me. i was the one who was supposed to be the charming host, the life of the party, but behind the scenes, i was a ticking time bomb, waiting to go off.

i caught myself checking the time for the umpteenth time, calculating the probability that my 'friends' would stay for the entire night, and what the consequences would be if they left early. it's like i'm stuck in this never-ending cycle of people-watching, predicting, and performance.

and then there's the small talk. ugh, the small talk. it's like i'm speaking a language that no one else understands, but somehow, everyone else is fluent in it. i feel like i'm just a prop, a pretend person, playing the role of the perfect friend, partner, or colleague, but with no actual substance or depth.

sometimes, i feel like i'm just pretending to be someone i'm not, and the more i try to fit in, the more i lose myself. it's like i'm living in a dream world, where everyone else is the real person, and i'm just a temporary visitor. 

i know it sounds crazy, but sometimes, i wonder if i'm even the one who's supposed to be in this room. if i'm just a ghost haunting the spaces of other people's lives. i know it's not rational, but when i'm in these situations, all i can think about is how fragile everything is, and how easily it can all fall apart.

i love people, i really do, but sometimes i feel like i'm just a conduit for other people's emotions, rather than having my own. it's exhausting, and it's lonely, and sometimes, i just want to scream.

attrs: {'persona': 'someone recently unemployed', 'trigger': 'an upcoming deadline or exam', 'register': 'short and blunt', 'length': 'long'}
text:
i've been staring at this spreadsheet all day, staring at these numbers staring back at me, and i feel like my whole world is unraveling. 2 weeks left till the deadline, and i still have no idea what i'm doing. 3 job offers, all of which i blew because i couldn't even be bothered to answer the phone when someone called. 
something's wrong with me, i know that. i'm like a ghost in my own skin, invisible to everyone else, but i can see the panic radiating off my face like a neon sign.  i try to put on a brave face, but beneath the surface, i'm screaming.  screaming at myself for not being good enough, for not being able to hold onto anything. 
i look around at my friends, and they all seem so sure of themselves, like they have some secret that i'll never know.  we were all friends back in college, but that was before they found their careers, their passions, their lives.  now they're just these different people, and i'm the one left behind, grasping at the edges of a world that's spinning without me. 
i've been trying to fill the void with work, but it's just a temporary distraction.  a Band-Aid on a bullet wound.  i feel like i'm just going through the motions, pretending to be something i'm not.  is anyone even looking for me?  or am i just invisible, a fleeting thought in someone's mind? 
i glance around the room, hoping to catch a glimpse of someone who'll understand, but everyone's too busy with their own lives.  i'm just a ghost, haunting the edges of reality, searching for a glimpse of connection in a world that's lost its meaning.  is this what it means to be alone?

attrs: {'persona': 'a person living alone in a new city', 'trigger': 'loneliness and isolation', 'register': 'hesitant, lots of qualifiers like "idk" and "maybe"', 'length': 'long'}
text:
i've been living here for a few months now, trying to start fresh after everything that went down back home. but it feels like i'm just going through the motions, you know? people say i'm adventurous, that i'm bold, but the truth is i'm just trying to fill a void.

idk, maybe it's the city that's the problem. it's so big and overwhelming, it feels like everyone's just trying to pass me by. i see people laughing and smiling, holding hands... and i feel this pang in my chest, like i'm just pretending to be a part of it all.

i've been trying to make friends, but it's hard when everyone's just so... perfect. they're all so put together, with their careers and their relationships and their 'stable' lives. i'm just over here, idk, maybe i'm just not cut out for this whole adult thing.

i had a girl, a few weeks ago. we met in a coffee shop, and for a second, i thought maybe this was it. maybe i'd finally found someone who understood me. but it was just a fling, you know? we talked for a few days, and then it was over. maybe it was because i messed it up, or maybe it was because she just wasn't that into me... but whatever it was, it left me feeling empty.

maybe i'm just not good at relationships. maybe i'm just not good at anything. i feel like i'm just drifting through life, without any real purpose. i try to hold onto things, but they always slip away from me. idk, maybe i'm just delusional, thinking i can ever really connect with someone.

it's funny, when i'm feeling anxious, i'll read all these self-help articles and inspirational quotes, and people will say things like "you are enough" and "you are worthy." but when i'm feeling empty, those same words just sound hollow. i guess what i'm trying to say is... maybe i'm just not whole, maybe i'm just a work in progress. idk, maybe that's just the problem.

attrs: {'persona': 'a night-shift worker', 'trigger': 'loneliness and isolation', 'register': 'long and rambling with run-on sentences', 'length': 'medium'}
text:
another 12 hours till dawn, the only solace in the darkness, the fluorescent hum a constant reminder that i'm not alone but i'm so so alone. i've been watching the world outside these walls for what feels like an eternity, the faces blurred and insignificant, the sounds muffled by the steel and concrete cage that confines me. i try to recall the last time someone smiled at me, the last time someone's eyes locked onto mine and i felt seen. but it's been so long, the faces and the smiles have started to fade, like watercolors in the rain.

i'm a ghost drifting through the void, a shadow of a person, a fleeting thought in the minds of those who pass by. i've given up trying to hold on to anything, to anyone, because it's all just a mirage, a temporary high that crashes and leaves me staring at the abyss. i'm just a momentary blip on the radar of the world, a whispered secret in the dead of night, a forgotten heartbeat in the stillness of the darkness. and in this isolation, i'm starting to lose myself, to unravel thread by thread, to become just another faceless soul lost in the labyrinth of the city.

text:
idk, i'm just really lost, i guess. i feel like i'm stuck in this perpetual limbo where nothing makes sense and everything feels like it's floating on uncertain ground. i keep wondering if i'm even making sense anymore, if i'm just some sort of figment of my own imagination. i know it sounds crazy, but that's how it feels.

today, i had this convo with my best friend emily and i just felt like i was talking to this stranger, you know? like, she's always been there for me, but now i'm not even sure if that's real or not. maybe she's just pretending, maybe i'm just pretending to care. it's all so fuzzy.

and then there's this guy, tyler, who's been trying to get close to me. at first, i thought he was really into me, like, for real. but now i'm not so sure. maybe i was just reading too much into it, maybe he's just being friendly. ugh, it's like my mind is constantly spinning and i don't know how to make it stop.

idk, what's the point of any of this? is it even worth trying to figure it out? maybe i'm just doomed to be this way forever. maybe i'm some kind of broken mirror, just reflecting shards of someone else's thoughts and feelings. that's what it feels like, anyway.

sometimes i feel like i'm just going through the motions, you know? like, i'm pretending to be this person, this student, this friend, but it's all just a facade. maybe i'm just hiding behind a mask and waiting for someone to come and tear it off. idk, maybe that's the only way to get real.

i don't know, maybe i'm just overthinking everything. maybe i should just relax and let things happen naturally. but then again, what if that's exactly what's happening? what if everything i think is happening is just my own twisted narrative. ugh, it's all just so confusing.

i guess that's all i have for now. not that it's anything new, anyway. just the same old feelings of uncertainty and fear. maybe someday i'll find my way out of this labyrinth, but until then, i'm stuck in this dark place, searching for a glimmer of hope.

attrs: {'persona': 'a university student in exam season', 'trigger': 'sleep problems', 'register': 'lowercase, hurried, minimal punctuation', 'length': 'long'}
text:
i'm writing this at 3 am, my eyes burning with exhaustion, but my mind racing like a runaway train. i don't know how much longer i can keep this up. exams are looming and i'm drowning in a sea of anxiety. but it's not just the academics that are getting to me. it's the feeling that i'm not even a real person anymore.

i look in the mirror and see a stranger's face staring back at me. the eyes are different, the smile is different. it's like i'm wearing a mask, but i'm not even sure if i know how to take it off. sometimes i feel like i'm just pretending to be someone i'm not, and the more i try to hold on to this person, the more they slip away.

i thought i had found someone who could see me, who could understand me. but they just walked away. left me standing in the dark, wondering if i was even worth the effort. it's like i'm always on the outside looking in, watching the world go by through a cracked window.

i try to fill the void with distractions - social media, video games, anything to keep my mind off the emptiness that's eating away at me. but it's like i'm just sweeping the floor, pushing the dust and dirt under the carpet. it's still there, waiting to pounce when i least expect it.

i'm so scared of being abandoned, of being left behind. i'll do anything to avoid that feeling. but it's like i'm trapped in a never-ending cycle. i attract people, i get close to them, and then i push them away. i don't know why. it's like i'm trying to prove something to myself, but i'm not even sure what that is.

i don't know how to break this cycle. i don't know how to stop being so empty. all i know is that i have to keep going, no matter how hard it gets. but the question is, am i even going in the right direction?

attrs: {'persona': 'a young professional', 'trigger': 'family conflict', 'register': 'short and blunt', 'length': 'long'}
text:
my family is a ticking time bomb and i'm the one holding the match. my mom is a master manipulator, playing the victim 24/7, while my dad is a cold, calculating machine who'd sell his own soul for a chance to be the "good" guy. but i'm the one who's always stuck in the middle, trying to mediate their never-ending cycle of anger and resentment.

today was a mess. we had dinner together and my mom started going off about how i never do enough to help her, while my dad just sat there, seething in silence. i tried to stay out of it, but my mom wouldn't let me be. she kept calling me "ungrateful" and " uncaring", making me feel like i'm the one who's broken.

and then, just as i thought things couldn't get any worse, my dad started talking about how he's "tired" of me, how i'm "always letting him down". i felt like i'd been punched in the gut. like i'm not good enough for either of them.

i know i'm the reason for all of this drama, but i don't know how to stop it. i feel like i'm living in a never-ending nightmare, where no matter what i do, i'll never be enough. my mom says i'm "too sensitive", that i need to toughen up, while my dad just says i'm "lazy", that i need to pull my weight.

i'm so sick of being the one who's always running on a hamster wheel, getting nowhere, feeling empty and meaningless. i feel like i'm disappearing, like i'm just a ghost hovering around the edges of my own life. i don't know who i am anymore, or what i want, or where i fit in. all i know is that i'm trapped in this never-ending cycle of hurt and anger, and i don't know how to escape.

i had this dream last night that my parents were gone, and i was all alone in this empty house. i woke up feeling scared and alone, like i really was abandoned. but then i thought, what if i'm the one who abandoned them? what if i'm just too much to handle?

attrs: {'persona': 'a university student in exam season', 'trigger': 'an anniversary of a hard event', 'register': 'lowercase, hurried, minimal punctuation', 'length': 'long'}
text:
today's the one year anniversary of i completely falling apart after josh left me for sarah. i thought we had forever, i guess i was wrong. i've been trying to move on, but every time i see a couple holding hands i'm hit with this wave of jealousy and i'm like what if this is what we would've had? what if i'm just not good enough? 

i've been talking to my friends, trying to convince myself that i'm better off without him, but every time we hang out and he's with someone new, i'm like a punch in the gut. i don't know if i can do this. i feel like i'm just existing, not really living. my grades are slipping, my parents are worried, and i just feel so lost.

i try to remind myself of all the good times we had, but they feel so fake now. like, yeah we had a lot of fun, but at what cost? he was just using me for his own happiness, and i was just a means to an end. i'm starting to realize that i've been holding onto him for way too long, just in case he comes crawling back.

but the truth is, i know he's not coming back. and even if he was, i'm not sure i'd want him. i'm not the same person i was when we were together. i've changed, but i'm not really sure who i am anymore. i feel like i'm just a shadow of my former self, and it's hard to find my own identity.

i guess what i'm trying to say is, i'm still hurting, still trying to figure out who i am without him. but maybe that's the problem - maybe i'm still clinging to him, still trying to prove myself to him. i don't know, maybe i just need to let go and move on. but it's hard when all i can think about is what could've been.

attrs: {'persona': 'a new parent', 'trigger': 'loneliness and isolation', 'register': 'short and blunt', 'length': 'long'}
text:
i'm just going through the motions of parenthood now, a facade of happiness masking the emptiness that's been eating away at me since the baby's birth. my husband thinks i'm happy, that i'm loving every minute of it, but the truth is i feel like a ghost hovering above my own life.

i try to put on a brave face for the parents' group, for the friends who all seem to have their lives together, but behind closed doors i'm a mess. i'm consumed by fears of abandonment, of being left behind when my child grows up and needs no one. it's a constant, gnawing voice in the back of my mind that threatens to derail me at any moment.

i have this cycle where i idealize people - friends, family, even strangers - and then promptly discard them once they inevitably disappoint me. it's like i'm addicted to the high of infatuation, but the crash is always brutal. my current friend group is a revolving door, people come and go as quickly as the seasons change.

i feel like i'm living in a dream, where one wrong move and i'll wake up to find it's all been a cruel joke. the pressure to be a perfect parent is suffocating me, and i'm trapped in this prison of my own making. i just wish i could scream and no one would hear me.

sometimes i look at my baby and wonder what they're going to be like when they're older. are they going to be like their dad, carefree and reckless? or are they going to be more like me, trapped in this labyrinth of my own insecurities? the thought sends a shiver down my spine, and for a moment i feel like i'm staring into the abyss.

i know i need to get help, but the fear of being seen as weak is paralyzing me. i feel like i'm walking a tightrope, one wrong step and i'll plummet to the ground. and when i finally work up the courage to reach out, will anyone be there to catch me?

i'm lost in this sea of uncertainty, and i don't know how to find my way back to solid ground. all i can do is keep moving forward, one step at a time, and hope that someday i'll find my footing again.

attrs: {'persona': 'a recent graduate living back home', 'trigger': 'family conflict', 'register': 'short and blunt', 'length': 'long'}
text:
family dinner turns into war every night. my parents just can't seem to agree on anything. my mom's always been the peacemaker but i can tell the tension's been eating away at her. my dad's been distant since the argument at grandma's last week. now he's acting like he's above all of this.

i hate how i react. one minute i'm calm, trying to stay out of it, the next i'm screaming at my parents like they're the ones who are crazy. it's like i'm possessed or something. my mom says i'm just a mirror for their emotions, that i'm not really feeling anything. but it hurts to hear that. it feels like she's saying i'm not even a real person.

i look around the table at my family and i see all these conflicting masks. my mom's the perfect daughter, my dad's the rugged hero, my brother's the lovable goofball. but when the facade cracks, i'm left with this void inside. it's like i'm made of dust and my entire existence is just a temporary shelter from the storm.

i keep getting sucked into these relationships where i'm the one who's always on edge, waiting for the other shoe to drop. i'm the one who's always the first to forgive, to forget, to get back into the game. but it feels like i'm just playing this exhausting game of emotional chess with strangers. do i ever get to be the one who's in control for once?

i guess that's the question i'm stuck on right now. is it possible to ever be the one who knows what they want, who knows what they need? or am i just a permanent guest in someone else's life, forever trapped in this dance of desperation?

attrs: {'persona': 'a new parent', 'trigger': 'moving to a new place', 'register': 'long and rambling with run-on sentences', 'length': 'long'}
text:
