i keep flipping back and forth between this vision of myself as a perfect new mom, the one with the perfect house and the perfect little family, and then i see the scary reality of it all - the messy diapers and the endless sleepless nights and the crippling loneliness that comes with moving to a new place alone i feel like i'm trapped in this endless cycle of shame and inadequacy

my mind is racing with all the what-ifs and the maybes - what if i'm not good enough? will my baby grow up with a daddy who's too busy to be there? what if i lose my mind trying to take care of everything on my own? it's like my brain is screaming at me to just give up and let the world just spin around without me

but then i look at my baby and i see this tiny little human who depends on me for everything - and for a second, i feel like i have no choice but to put on this mask of perfection and just pretend like everything is okay i mean, who needs the real me when i can just be the perfect parent to this tiny little soul?

but the fear of being found out, of being a failure, of being abandoned, it's like a constant whisper in my ear - "you're not good enough, you're not good enough" and it's eating away at me, it's eating away at my very sense of self

attrs: {'persona': 'a night-shift worker', 'trigger': 'money problems', 'register': 'short and blunt', 'length': 'long'}
text:
i'm lost in the city of broken promises. my phone's dead, and i've got a month's rent due tomorrow. no one wants to hear it, not my bosses, not my parents, not my so-called friends. they all want a piece of me, but they don't give anything back. they just take and take, like they're sucking the life out of me.

sometimes i wonder if i'm just a pawn in their game, a spare part they can discard when they're done with me. i see it in their eyes, the way they look at me like i'm nothing but a fleeting distraction. a means to an end. a way to pass the time until something more interesting comes along.

i try to convince myself that i'm not that person. that i'm not just a disposable thing, a thing that can be broken and discarded. but the fear creeps in, like a cold wind on a winter's night. it tells me that i'm not good enough, that i'll never be good enough. that i'm just a fragile thing, waiting to shatter under the weight of someone else's expectations.

i thought i was doing okay, that i was rising above it all. but the truth is, i'm still stuck in the mud, still swimming in quicksand. i'm still trying to find a lifeline, but they just pull me under, and i'm left gasping for air.

i hate it when the world tells me i'm not enough. i hate it when i believe them. i hate it when i lose myself in the darkness, when i lose my grip on reality. but it's hard to resist the void, when it's all that's left. and so i slip away, into the shadows, and lose myself in the endless expanse of nothingness.

attrs: {'persona': 'someone recently unemployed', 'trigger': 'family conflict', 'register': 'long and rambling with run-on sentences', 'length': 'long'}
text:
i feel like i'm disappearing piece by piece, like the fragments of my own existence are being pulled apart by the undertow of my family's anger. they say i'm lazy, that i'm good for nothing, that i'm the reason we're falling apart. but what do they really know?

i try to fill the void they've created, to distract myself from the crushing silence that's all that's left. i take long walks, trying to shake the feeling that i'm invisible, that no one truly sees me. but even the fresh air can't wash away the taste of betrayal.

my mom's words still linger, echoing in my mind like a mantra of self-doubt. "you'll never amount to anything, just like your dad." my dad, who's actually doing just fine, thank you very much. he's the one they blame me for, for not being him. like i can just switch places with him or something.

anyway, i guess that's why i was so shocked when they kicked me out. i thought we were working through our issues, that we were finally starting to heal. but i guess that was just a facade. now i'm left with nothing, just a dwindling bank account and a sense of utter emptiness.

i look around at all these people on social media, posting about their perfect lives, their happy families... it just makes me feel like a failure. like i'm just a joke, a sideshow attraction. i wonder if they ever feel this way, this lost and alone, or are they just too busy pretending to be someone they're not.

i guess what i'm trying to say is that i'm not a failure. i'm not a joke. i'm just... lost. and scared. and hurt. and alone. and trying to figure out who i am, without the people who are supposed to love me.

attrs: {'persona': 'a middle-aged caregiver', 'trigger': 'sleep problems', 'register': 'lowercase, hurried, minimal punctuation', 'length': 'long'}
text:
i feel like i'm just going through the motions of being a wife and mother, but nobody really knows what's going on behind closed doors. i put on a brave face for the kids, for my husband, but at night it all feels like a weight crushing me. 

sometimes i wonder if they even know the real me. do they see the exhausted shell that stares back at them in the mirror, the one who's barely holding on to the fragments of her life. i try to hide it from them, to pretend that everything is okay, but it's like i'm living in a dream world and they're the only ones who can wake me up.

but then there's the feeling of emptiness that follows me everywhere. it's like i'm a ghost hovering above my own life, watching myself go through the motions without any sense of purpose or direction. i feel like i'm just waiting for something to happen, for someone to notice me, to care about me. but it never comes.

my husband says i'm just stressed, that i need to take some time for myself, but he doesn't get it. he doesn't see the fears that creep in every night, the ones that tell me i'm not good enough, that i'm just a failure waiting to happen. he doesn't see the emptiness that eats away at me, the feeling that i'm just going through the motions of being a wife and mother.

i try to talk to him about it, but he just gets defensive, tells me i'm being too sensitive. and the kids, they don't know what to make of it. they're too young to understand, to see the cracks in the facade that i put on for them. they just think their mom is sad, that i need to be stronger, to be braver. but they don't know the truth. they don't know what it's like to feel like you're living someone else's life, like you're just a shadow of the person you used to be.

attrs: {'persona': 'a young professional', 'trigger': 'loneliness and isolation', 'register': 'lowercase, hurried, minimal punctuation', 'length': 'long'}
text:
i've been seeing so many couples on instagram, laughing and smiling and toasting champagne. and i'm over here feeling like i'm just going to end up alone, watching everyone else live their best lives. i know i'm not that person, but it's hard not to compare when all i see are photos of people who seem to have their shit together.
i went out with a guy the other night, he seemed perfect at first, really cute and charming. we talked for hours, and i felt like i was connecting with him on a real level. but when it got late, he just kinda... disappeared. no texts, no calls, no nothing. i felt like i was just a fling to him, not even worth remembering.
and then there's my family, always on my case about something. i know i can be distant sometimes, but i'm just trying to protect myself from getting hurt. they never get it, they never understand that i'm just trying to keep my guard up. they think i'm just being aloof, but really i'm just trying to keep my heart from getting broken.
i feel like i'm stuck in this never-ending cycle of idealizing people and then discarding them. it's like i'm just a magnet for toxic relationships. and it's exhausting, it's so draining. i feel like i'm just pretending to be a normal person, but really i'm just a mess.
i tried to tell myself that i'm just being too picky, that i need to open myself up to new people and experiences. but the truth is, i'm just scared. i'm scared of getting hurt, of getting close to someone and then having them walk away. i'm scared of being alone again. but at the same time, i'm terrified of being stuck in this lonely rut forever.
i don't know what's wrong with me, i really don't. i feel like i'm just a hot mess of emotions and fears and doubts. i just wish someone could understand me, just for once. i wish someone could see past all the facade and just see the real me, flaws and all.

attrs: {'persona': 'a recently divorced person', 'trigger': 'a health scare', 'register': 'short and blunt', 'length': 'long'}
text:
can't even begin to piece myself together right now. doc's still running tests, but the waiting is killing me. i feel like i'm stuck in some kind of neverending nightmare. every time i think i'm seeing a glimmer of light at the end of the tunnel, it just keeps moving away.

people keep asking me how i'm doing, but honestly, i don't know. i feel like i'm just pretending to be fine. i'm not. i'm lost and alone and i have no idea how to find my way back to myself. every time i try to talk to someone, i just end up sounding like a whiny brat. and it's exhausting trying to put on a mask all the time.

i keep thinking about my ex, wondering what i did wrong and why he's so gone. did i not love him enough? was it something i said? i feel like i'm haunted by my own doubts and fears. every time i see someone new, i'm like a puppy with a raw bone - i'll do anything to get them to like me, to love me. but it never lasts. they see the real me and they're gone.

i'm terrified of being abandoned, of being left behind like a used tissue. it's what happened with my ex, and i don't know how to shake it off. every time we had a fight, i'd get so desperate and clingy, and he'd just shut me down. it was like i was suffocating him. but i couldn't help it. i didn't know how.

doc says i'm going through a major crisis, but what does that even mean? i feel like i'm losing myself all over again. i've been in therapy for years, but it feels like i'm just going through the motions. i need something more. i need to find a way to stop being so broken, to stop being so scared. but what if i'm too broken to fix myself? what if i'm just a fragile, flawed, failed human being, forever stuck in this abyss?

attrs: {'persona': 'a middle-aged caregiver', 'trigger': 'a health scare', 'register': 'hesitant, lots of qualifiers like "idk" and "maybe"', 'length': 'long'}
text:
i feel like i'm stuck in this never-ending cycle of love and rejection, idk why but every time i let someone in, i'm terrified they'll break me all over again. my heart's like a canvas, always switching between vibrant colors and a dull gray. maybe it's the stress of caring for my family, idk, but every time someone needs me, i feel like i'm drowning.

my husband left me for a younger woman, supposedly it was just a mistake, but the pain lingered, like an open wound that refused to close. i got so caught up in trying to prove myself to him that i forgot who i was, idk, outside of being the partner, the caregiver, the mother figure. now, every time i see him with his new 'perfect' life, i feel like i'm staring into a stranger's eyes, and it's just so...empty.

today, the doctor said i need to slow down, take it easy, but how can i do that when my family's depending on me? maybe i'm just not good enough, maybe i'll never be enough. the thought sends a shiver down my spine, and for a moment, i wonder if i'm just a temporary fix, a Band-Aid on a bullet wound.

i look around at my kids, and they need me, they depend on me, but what happens when they grow up and leave the nest? will i be left with nothing but this hollow feeling, this sense of inadequacy? idk, maybe i'm just being paranoid, but the thought of being alone, truly alone, is a prospect that keeps me up at night, staring at the ceiling, wondering who i am, and what i'm supposed to be doing here.

attrs: {'persona': 'a night-shift worker', 'trigger': 'family conflict', 'register': 'short and blunt', 'length': 'long'}
text:
ive been working the night shift for months now, just me and the dim hum of the machines. its weird how fast youre used to the silence, but when my family comes over on the weekends, it feels like theyre speaking in a different language. my sister thinks im selfish for not wanting to spend my days off with them, but what about my own sanity? i need the quiet to think, to process the crap thats been eating away at me.

but of course, they dont get it. they dont understand why i get so worked up when they criticize me for not being more present. its like they think i just need to put on a smile and pretend everything is fine, but the truth is, i dont know who i am without them. and thats what scares me - that if i lose them, if i lose myself, ill be left with nothing.

my mom was over yesterday, and she kept saying i need to find my passion, my purpose. but what about my love for the quiet? what about the comfort of my own company? does that not count? i feel like i m torn between two worlds, two identities. one is the sleep-deprived, anxiety-ridden mess i am, and the other is the person my family wants me to be.

its like i m stuck in this never-ending cycle of idealization and rejection. i fall for someone, and they get under my skin, but then they leave me and ill be left to pick up the pieces. its a sick game, and im tired of playing. i just want to be left alone, to figure out who i am without all the drama and expectation. but how do i do that when its all i know is how to react to the chaos that surrounds me?

attrs: {'persona': 'a person living alone in a new city', 'trigger': 'money problems', 'register': 'short and blunt', 'length': 'long'}
text:
just got to the city, emptying my pockets for the umpteenth time. i swear, my future is just a series of fleeting prospects, each one more meaningless than the last. 
i've been trying to make friends here, but it feels like every new face is a threat, a constant reminder that i'm just a temporary distraction from someone else's life. 
i saw this girl at the coffee shop yesterday and we hit it off, but today she texted me saying she was just being friendly. what even is that? 
i guess i'm just a placeholder until she figures out what she really wants. 
i've been here for weeks, and it's like i'm just a temporary resident, not even sure how long i'll be here before i'm tossed aside. 
the worst part is, i don't even feel like i have a real life here. i'm just going through the motions, trying to make enough to keep a roof over my head and a meal in my belly. 
i'm tired of being invisible, tired of being someone's afterthought. 
but at the same time, i'm terrified of being needed. 
i've been on so many dates, and every single one of them has ended in disaster. 
i'm starting to think it's because i'm not really sure who i am, or what i want. 
i'm just a chameleon, changing colors to blend in with whoever will take me in for a little while. 
but what happens when the colors fade? 
i feel like i'm living in someone else's skin, just waiting for the inevitable moment when they'll realize they can do without me. 
is this what it means to be alone, or is it just a state of mind? 
i don't know anymore. all i know is that i'm running out of time, and i don't know how much longer i can keep pretending to be someone i'm not.

attrs: {'persona': 'someone recently unemployed', 'trigger': 'social situations', 'register': 'lowercase, hurried, minimal punctuation', 'length': 'long'}
text:
i was at a party tonight and everyone was being so nice to me, laughing and asking about my job and stuff. i thought maybe i finally found my tribe, you know, people who like me for who i am. but then we got to the part where we were all taking pictures and i saw that my ex was there. and she was being super sweet to me, smiling and flirting like nothing ever happened. and for a second i felt like i was the only one who saw it, like i was the only one who knew that she was just pretending to be friendly.

but then i saw my friends from work staring at me, and they were all just chatting and laughing like nothing ever happened with my ex. and i realized that they didn't see it, that they didn't know what was going on. and it felt like i was the only one who was still hurting, like i was the only one who still cared.

i tried to play it cool, to just pretend like everything was fine, but it wasn't. it was like my whole world had shifted again. and now i'm just sitting here, wondering if i'll ever be able to trust anyone, or if i'll just be stuck in this never-ending cycle of being loved and then being rejected.

i feel so empty right now, like i'm just a ghost hovering around people's lives. i try to connect with them, but it's all just a facade. and i'm starting to wonder if anyone will ever truly see me, or if i'll just be stuck in this limbo forever.

i keep thinking about what my therapist said, about how i need to learn to trust myself, to trust my own feelings and instincts. but how can i do that when i'm constantly being pulled in different directions? when everyone i care about is just using me, or discarding me like I'm nothing?

i just feel so lost right now, like i'm wandering around in the dark with no direction or purpose. and i don't know how to find my way out of this darkness, or how to stop being so broken.

attrs: {'persona': 'a night-shift worker', 'trigger': 'social situations', 'register': 'short and blunt', 'length': 'long'}
text:
i got called out by coworkers at the graveyard shift about my 'mood swings' again. i know i get upset when things don't go my way, but it's hard when every conversation feels like a minefield. i swear, people just don't understand me.

sometimes i feel like i'm a different person in the daytime. everyone's so 'me' and 'out there' but me, i'm just a void. i'm the only one who's still awake, wondering what it's all about. but when i'm around others, they just see the surface. they don't see the whirlpool inside me that's always spinning.

i tried to make small talk with that new guy in the break room, but he asked me how i was doing and i just broke down. i told him i'm still trying to figure that out. he just looked at me with that empathetic face and i felt like he saw right through me. but was it really him seeing the real me, or was it just his idea of what it means to be sympathetic?

i hate it when people say 'you're so intense' or 'you're really passionate'. what they don't understand is that i'm just a mess. i'm trying to put together pieces of myself that keep getting torn apart. i'm not even sure what the pieces are or where they came from.

people always talk about how they're 'good listeners' or 'supportive friends'. but when i'm with them, i feel like they're just waiting for me to say something so they can swoop in and save the day. newsflash: i don't need saving. i need someone to just sit with me in the quiet and not try to fix everything.

i swear, the more i try to put myself together, the more i fall apart. it's like i'm living in a perpetual state of chaos and no one gets it except for maybe my mom. she's always been the one person who didn't try to fix me or tell me what i should be doing. but even with her, i feel like i'm still trying to prove myself to her. it's exhausting.

but its all so fragile its like one wrong move and the whole thing comes crashing down and i know it will because i've been doing this dance for so long i know every step and every move and every word that i say and every smile that i put on my face its all just a facade a disguise a costume that im wearing to hide the emptiness from everyone else but not from myself

i feel like im living with this stranger who just happens to be wearing my skin and im not even sure what she likes or what she wants or what makes her tick is it all just a bunch of nonsense that im pretending to care about or is there something more beneath the surface something that only i can hear if i really listen

my friends all say im amazing and i believe them for a second but then i start to doubt myself and wonder if theyre just being nice or if they really see me or if im just a phase for them and then i'm back to square one where im not even sure who im supposed to be anymore

i keep thinking about all the people who arent here with me anymore and how much it hurts to not have them here to share this new chapter of my life with and it feels like a part of me is missing and i dont know if i can ever find it again or if it even exists in the first place i guess i just have to keep moving forward and hoping that someday i'll figure it out for myself but till then im just stuck in this limbo where nothing makes sense and everything feels like its falling apart

i keep flinging myself at people, throwing myself into relationships, hoping that this time will be different. maybe this time, i'll finally find someone who sees me for who i truly am. but it never seems to work out. people get tired of my drama, my constant need for validation. and then i'm left feeling empty and alone.

i don't know who i am anymore. i don't know what i want or what i need. all i know is that i'm tired, so tired of feeling like i'm living in someone else's skin. i'm tired of being the person everyone else wants me to be, not the person i actually am. maybe someday i'll figure out who i am, but until then, i'm stuck in this never-ending cycle of self-doubt and uncertainty.

after it was over, i sent him a text, and he basically ghosted me. i guess i should've known better, but it stung. i'm starting to feel like i'm just not good enough for anyone. like, i'll get close to someone, and then they'll just discard me for someone better. maybe that's just how it is. maybe i'm just not worthy of love.

sometimes i feel like i'm living in this constant state of limbo, where everything is possible, but nothing is real. i'm trying to hold on to my old self, but it feels like she's slipping away from me. idk, maybe i'm just anxious and stressed out, but it feels like my whole identity is crumbling beneath my feet. i don't know who i am, or what i want, or where i'm going. maybe that's the problem. maybe i'm just lost, and i don't know how to find my way back to myself.

text:

i try to tell myself its just move on and that new people are waiting around the corner, but honestly, i just cant shake this sense of failure. did i do something wrong? was i not enough? ugh, my mind is a mess and i just wanna crawl under a rock and hide.

i went on a date last night and it was literally the most perfect night ever. we laughed, we danced, we connected on a level that felt so real. but when he woke up this morning and told me he was tired and didn't want to talk, my whole world came crashing down.

i know, i know, i'm just being dramatic, but honestly, it felt like he was abandoning me all over again. and its not just that. its like i'm living in this constant state of limbo where every relationship i have with someone is always simultaneously the best thing that's ever happened to me and also the worst thing that's ever happened to me.

like, i love him, but i hate him. and i hate myself for loving him so much. and then i'll get to a point where i'll think maybe this time it'll be different, maybe this time he'll stay, but then he'll do the same thing again and i'll be left wondering if i'm just delusional or if he really does have some kind of superpower that lets him just waltz in and out of my life.

i guess what i'm trying to say is, i dont know who i am anymore. i feel like i'm just a shell of a person, a fake, a pretender. i dont know what i want or who i am or what i believe in. all i know is that i'm scared and alone and i dont know how to stop feeling this way

attrs: {'persona': 'a person living alone in a new city', 'trigger': 'moving to a new place', 'register': 'long and rambling with run-on sentences', 'length': 'long'}
text:
moving to a new city has been like a rebirth for me, or so i thought. the city feels so clean and empty, like a fresh slate waiting for me to leave my mark. but the more i explore, the more i realize how lost i am. the streets all look the same, the faces too. everyone seems so put together, so in control. it's like i'm staring at a photograph, trying to remember what it looks like.

i've been trying to make friends, but it's like reaching for a mirage. they seem so real at first, but then they vanish into thin air. i feel like a ghost hovering on the periphery of their lives. i try to hold on, to cling to these tentative connections, but they always seem to slip away. leaving me with nothing but the echoes of what could have been.

sometimes i feel like i'm living two lives, one where i'm a successful, confident person, and the other where i'm a scared, alone mess. the fear of abandonment is always there, lurking in the shadows, whispering nasty things in my ear. what if i get left behind? what if no one needs me? the questions swirl in my head like a maelstrom, making it hard to breathe.

i look around at the city, and it feels like a prison. every face i see is a reminder that i'm not good enough, that i'll never find what i'm looking for. the silence is deafening, a constant reminder that i'm invisible. and the emptiness... it's like a hollow cavity inside me, aching to be filled. but i don't know how.

i feel like i'm searching for a ghost, a reflection of myself that i can cling to. but it's always just out of reach. i'm left with the fragments of what could have been, the shattered remains of my own identity. and the fear that i'll never find my way back to myself.

attrs: {'persona': 'a person living alone in a new city', 'trigger': 'sleep problems', 'register': 'long and rambling with run-on sentences', 'length': 'long'}
text:
i just spent the last hour staring at my walls, wondering what im doing here. another city, another apartment, another attempt at a life that feels like its been painted on a canvas by someone else. people keep telling me to 'find myself' but what even is that anymore? am i just a collection of fleeting moments, a patchwork of memories stitched together by threads of anxiety and regret?

i cant sleep because my mind is racing, conjuring up scenarios where i wake up tomorrow to find that everything i thought i knew is a lie. my phone is blowing up with notifications, reminders that i have to go to work, to pay bills, to buy groceries. but what is the point of any of it? is it all just a facade, a masquerade ball where im forced to put on a mask of happiness and sanity when in reality im just numb and hollow?

i try to fill the void with distractions - TV, video games, endless scrolling through social media. but nothing sticks, nothing grabs hold and refuses to let go. its like i'm a ghost, invisible and intangible, drifting through the world without any sense of purpose or belonging. and when the darkness closes in and i feel like i'm drowning, i reach out for someone, anyone, to pull me to safety.

but they always drop me. they say i'm too much, too needy, too unpredictable. and then they're gone, leaving me to face the abyss alone. why do i keep doing this to myself? why do i keep putting myself out there, only to be crushed by the weight of my own expectations?

i look around at the unfamiliar walls, the strange furniture, and the city that never sleeps. and i wonder, what am i even doing here? is this who i am, or is this just a role i'm playing? i dont know anymore. all i know is that i need to get out of here, to escape the prison of my own mind before it consumes me whole.

attrs: {'persona': 'a night-shift worker', 'trigger': 'an anniversary of a hard event', 'register': 'short and blunt', 'length': 'medium'}
text:
tonight's the anniversary of the night she walked out. a year's passed but the pain's still here, like a wound that won't heal. i replay the conversations in my head, wondering what i did wrong. maybe it was the way i was, or the way i wasn't. either way, it ended. and now she's just a memory, a ghost haunting the edges of my mind.

i see her in every fleeting glance, in the way the shadows dance on the wall. i reach for my phone, scrolling through old messages, searching for answers. but the screen's just a blur of words, a reminder of the silence that followed.

i'm stuck in this cycle, chasing the highs, clinging to the lows. it's a delicate balance, one wrong move and i'm left with nothing. the emptiness feels like a living thing, a constant companion that refuses to leave. i'm tired of being the one who's always waiting, always hoping. where's the safety in that?

i'm so sick of being invisible, of being a ghost that people can't see or touch. but how do i become visible when everyone i love is slowly disappearing from my life?

attrs: {'persona': 'a recent graduate living back home', 'trigger': 'sleep problems', 'register': 'lowercase, hurried, minimal punctuation', 'length': 'long'}
text:
i dont know what i am anymore. every morning i wake up and my whole world shifts. one minute im feeling like i'm finally getting my life together and the next im drowning in anxiety and uncertainty.

i cant sleep. cant sleep because my brain is racing a million miles an hour. is this really my life or am i just pretending it is. did i really quit my job or did i just give up on my dreams. is my boyfriend really into me or is he just being nice.

my parents are always on my case about something. they dont understand me. they think i'm just being lazy or not trying hard enough. but they have no idea what its like to be me. they have no idea what its like to be lost and scared and alone all the time.

i feel like im living in this bubble where nothing makes sense. where every conversation is a struggle and every relationship is a lie. did i really just get into that fight with my mom or was it just a dream. do i really have friends or am i just pretending to be part of a group.

sometimes i catch myself talking to people like they're not real. like i'm just going through the motions and pretending to be interested. it gets to the point where i wonder if any of it is real. am i just a character in my own story or am i really living.

i dont know who i am anymore. i dont know what i want. i dont know what i need. all i know is that i need to get out of here. out of this place. out of this life. i need to start fresh. i need to start over. i just dont know where to start.

i feel so empty inside. so hollow. so alone. it feels like im just going through the motions of life without any real purpose or meaning. without any real direction. it feels like im just floating through life without any real connection to reality

attrs: {'persona': 'a middle-aged caregiver', 'trigger': 'family conflict', 'register': 'hesitant, lots of qualifiers like "idk" and "maybe"', 'length': 'long'}
text:
idk if i'm just being crazy or what but every time my family comes over for dinner, i feel like they're all waiting for something to go wrong. maybe it's just my imagination but i swear they're all just pretending to be polite or whatever. my brother's always making snide comments and my sister's always "forgetting" to bring her plate or something. and don't even get me started on my mom. she's always giving me this stuffy lecture about how i need to "pull myself together" or some nonsense like that.

i try to brush it off, but deep down i know they're all just using me to fulfill their own emotional voids. like, i'll be over there making them their favorite meal or something, and my brother will just go and insult me for no reason or my sister will start crying because she's "too tired" to hang out with me, and my mom will just sit there looking all superior and disapproving. it's like, i get it, they're all messed up, but do they have to take it out on me?

i don't know what's worse, though - the fact that they're all so emotionally unavailable, or the fact that i'm starting to believe it's all my fault. maybe if i just tried harder, maybe if i was more "helpful" or whatever, they'd actually start to care. but then again, maybe that's just my own projection. maybe they do care, but they just don't know how to show it.

anyway, after this whole ordeal, i'm just feeling really overwhelmed and unsure of myself. like, who am i, really? am i just some kind of emotional servant to this crazy family of mine? or am i actually worth something? idk, maybe i'm just being too sensitive or whatever. but honestly, it's hard not to wonder when it feels like everyone around me is just waiting for me to crack so they can pounce on me and "save me".

attrs: {'persona': 'someone recently unemployed', 'trigger': 'money problems', 'register': 'short and blunt', 'length': 'medium'}

i try to play it cool, but inside i'm dying. i keep telling myself i'm better off without her, that i deserve someone who actually cares. but deep down, i know that's just a lie. i've been down this road before, and it always ends in the same place - alone and broke.

why can't i catch a break? why can't i just be worthy of someone's love and acceptance for once? i swear, every time i think i've found someone, they just end up discarding me like trash. is it something i'm doing? am i just too broken to be loved?

attrs: {'persona': 'a new parent', 'trigger': 'an upcoming deadline or exam', 'register': 'short and blunt', 'length': 'long'}
text:

i feel like i've lost my sense of identity, like i'm just pretending to be someone i'm not. my partner is always telling me i'm an amazing mom, but what about who i am outside of this? do i still exist? do i still have my own interests, my own passions? it feels like i've been erased.

and to make matters worse, i have this looming deadline for my project at work. my boss is breathing down my neck and i'm feeling like i'm failing, like i'm not good enough. it's like this huge weight is crushing me and i don't know how to breathe.

i look at my baby and i see this tiny, perfect person who needs me to be strong. but what about me? do i need them to be strong too? am i strong? the more i think about it, the more i realize i have no idea who i am anymore.

i just want to be able to look in the mirror and see a person who's not a mess. but for now, i'm just stuck in this rut, trying to figure out who i am and what i want. and it's scary, because i don't know if i'll ever find my way out of this darkness.

attrs: {'persona': 'a recent graduate living back home', 'trigger': 'loneliness and isolation', 'register': 'lowercase, hurried, minimal punctuation', 'length': 'long'}
text: i don't know who i am without them but that's exactly what scares me, being alone is like drowning in a sea of nothingness and i thought i could just wade through it but it's a river that keeps changing course, pulling me under and i'm just along for the ride, trying to grab onto something familiar but it's all just a illusion, a dream that vanishes the moment i wake up, and i wake up and i wake up and it never ends, i've tried to cling to people but it's just a game of jenga, one wrong move and the whole thing comes crashing down and i'm left standing there, wondering who i am without them, without the constant need to be loved and validated, to be the center of their universe, to be the reason they get out of bed in the morning, it's exhausting, it's suffocating, it's like i'm living in a bubble and i don't know how to pop it, how to let go of the need to be perfect, to be whole, to be complete, because as long as i'm with them, i'm complete, but as soon as i'm alone, i'm just a hollow shell of a person, a nobody, a ghost wandering through life, searching for a purpose, a meaning, something to cling to, something to fill the void that's been growing inside me for so long, and it's getting harder and harder to find anything that's not tainted by my fear of abandonment, my fear of being left behind, my fear of being forgotten, and it's suffocating me, it's crushing me, it's making me feel like i'm going to break, like i'm going to shatter into a million pieces and there's nothing i can do to stop it, nothing i can do to make it stop.

attrs: {'persona': 'a middle-aged caregiver', 'trigger': 'an anniversary of a hard event', 'register': 'hesitant, lots of qualifiers like "idk" and "maybe"', 'length': 'long'}
text:
tonight marks the 5 year anniversary of his departure. idk how i'm feeling about it, maybe a mix of sadness and relief. we were happy, or so i thought. i mean, we had our ups and downs, but i convinced myself that i'd learned to manage, to keep the demons at bay.

looking back, i realize how fragile our relationship was. i'd idealize him, build him up in my mind, only to have him crumble when he couldn't meet my expectations. maybe it was my fault, maybe it was his. idk. all i know is that he walked away, leaving me with this hollow feeling.

i've tried to fill the void with work, with volunteering, with hobbies. but nothing seems to stick. maybe it's because i'm just pretending to be someone i'm not. idk. i feel like i'm living a lie, like i'm just going through the motions.

sometimes i'll see him in my dreams, and i'll wake up feeling like i've been punched in the gut. i know it's not real, but it's hard to shake the feeling that a part of me is still with him. idk what i'm supposed to do with these feelings.

i guess i'm still trying to figure out who i am without him. it's like i'm trying to assemble a puzzle with missing pieces. idk what the finished picture looks like, or if it even exists. all i know is that i'm scared, and i'm lost, and i'm not sure how to find my way back to myself.

i keep wondering if he ever thought about me, if he ever missed me. idk. maybe he's moved on, maybe he's found someone new. maybe that's for the best. but a part of me can't help but feel like i'll never be whole again.

attrs: {'persona': 'a person living alone in a new city', 'trigger': 'a health scare', 'register': 'short and blunt', 'length': 'medium'}
text:
i just got out of the hospital and everythin feels off. they say im stable now but the doc just smiled and patted me on the back - im not convinced. im stuck in this new city with no one to turn to. i keep thinking bout her, the one who left, the one who was mine. i idealized her, loved her with every fiber of my being. but then she disappeared without a word, just like she always does.

now im back to square one, a ghost haunting these streets. i see people passin by, laughing and happy, and it just fills me with emptiness. i try to make small talk with strangers but its all just a facade, a temporary reprieve from the crushing loneliness. sometimes i wonder if anyone would even notice if i vanished. do i deserve it? is it even possible for me to find someone who actually cares?

attrs: {'persona': 'a high-school student', 'trigger': 'sleep problems', 'register': 'lowercase, hurried, minimal punctuation', 'length': 'long'}
text:
tonight i stayed up all night watching the storm outside my window, trying to distract myself from the unease in my chest. we're moving away in 2 weeks and i just can't shake the feeling that this is all just a nightmare i'm trapped in. everyone keeps telling me i'm going to love our new life, that it'll be an adventure, but what if i don't? what if i'm just going to be another face in the crowd, another invisible person in a sea of strangers?

my mom says i'm being dramatic, that i'll make new friends and that it'll all be okay, but i know it's not that simple. i've seen how quickly people get rid of me when they don't need me anymore. it's like i'm toxic or something. i try to shake off these thoughts but they linger, haunting me in the shadows of my room.

i look around my room and i don't recognize anyone in this space. the posters on the wall, the books on the shelf, even the clothes in my closet all feel like someone else's. who am i really? am i just a collection of memories and experiences or am i something more?

i glance over at my diary and i see all the names i've crossed out, all the words i've scribbled out in frustration. it's like i'm constantly rewriting myself, trying to erase the parts of me that i don't want to be. but no matter how hard i try, these pieces always seem to seep back in, like blood through a wound.

i feel so empty, so hollow. i'm just a ghost haunting these halls, waiting for something to make me whole again. but what if it's just a dream? what if i'm just a dream and this whole thing is just a cruel joke? i don't know anymore, all i know is that i'm scared and alone and the storm outside is just a reflection of the storm inside me.

attrs: {'persona': 'someone recently unemployed', 'trigger': 'social situations', 'register': 'short and blunt', 'length': 'medium'}
text:
i'm lost in the limbo of small talk. everyone else has a place, a purpose. but not me. i'm just drifting, a ghost in the crowd. people try to connect, but i'm just a facade, a mask i wear when the masks of others are off.

i met her at the coffee shop today. we chatted for hours, laughed together, and i felt seen. for a moment, i thought maybe this was it. maybe i'd found someone who'd look beyond the surface and see the real me. but as we parted ways, she barely remembered my name. i'm starting to think that's just the way it goes. people forget. they forget the ones who are nobody.

i don't know what i want, but i know what i don't want. i don't want to be invisible. i don't want to be alone. but then i get scared, scared that someone will see me, really see me, and leave. i don't know how to stop the oscillation between craving connection and dreading rejection. it's like my mind is a seesaw, and i'm the only one who knows how to tip it.

attrs: {'persona': 'a middle-aged caregiver', 'trigger': 'loneliness and isolation', 'register': 'long and rambling with run-on sentences', 'length': 'medium'}
text:
i'm lost in the maze of my own mind, chasing shadows that only appear when no one's looking. the faces of my loved ones are forever etched in my memory, but the more i cling to them, the more they slip through my fingers like sand in the hourglass. i'm a jigsaw puzzle with missing pieces, and every time i try to find my place, i realize i'm just a fragile shard of a human being, waiting to be swept away by the tide of reality.

i think of all the people who've left me in the past, and the weight of their abandonment still settles on my shoulders like a shroud. my mind whirs with thoughts of what i did wrong, what i could've done differently, and why they left me to face the crushing loneliness alone. but when i'm alone, i'm haunted by the ghosts of my own making, whispers of a love that's lost, and the unbearable silence that follows.

i'm torn between holding on and letting go, between the comfort of familiarity and the thrill of the unknown. i'm a chameleon, shifting colors to blend in, but beneath the surface, i'm a hollow shell, waiting to be filled by someone, anyone, who can love me for who i am, imperfections and all

attrs: {'persona': 'a high-school student', 'trigger': 'family conflict', 'register': 'long and rambling with run-on sentences', 'length': 'medium'}
text:
i feel like i'm drowning in a sea of broken promises and fading dreams, with my family at the center of the storm. they say i'm too much to handle, that i'm a black hole of emotions that will suck everything in and destroy it. but is it true? or are they just scared of what they can't understand?

mom says i need to toughen up, to stop being so sensitive, but dad just shakes his head and says i'm too fragile. it's like they're speaking different languages, and i'm the one caught in the middle. sometimes i feel like i'm just a ghost hovering between two worlds, invisible and irrelevant.

i try to cling to the people who care about me, but they just pull away, afraid of getting too close. it's like i'm a magnet, drawing people in with promises of love and connection, only to repel them with my own fears and doubts. what if i'm just a fake, a pretend person trying to navigate this crazy world without a map? or what if i'm the real deal, and they're just too blind to see it?

attrs: {'persona': 'a person living alone in a new city', 'trigger': 'pressure at work or school', 'register': 'long and rambling with run-on sentences', 'length': 'medium'}
text: i've been trying to settle into this new city for weeks now, but everything feels so... off. my coworkers are all so perfect and put together, it's like they're trying to one up each other in a never ending game of who can be more competent. me? i'm just a hot mess, always second guessing myself and worrying that i'm going to fail at my job somehow. i try to talk to people, but they just seem to disappear on me, like they were never even there in the first place. i'm starting to think that i'm just invisible, that no one actually cares about me or my feelings. i saw this guy at the coffee shop the other day and we hit it off, but by the time i got home, i realized i had no idea what his name was or what we were even talking about. did i really just imagine the whole thing? i feel so alone in this city, like i'm just a ghost drifting through the streets, invisible and insignificant. sometimes i wonder if it's even worth trying to make friends or connections at all, because in the end, i'll just end up losing them anyway

attrs: {'persona': 'a new parent', 'trigger': 'moving to a new place', 'register': 'short and blunt', 'length': 'long'}
text:
i just woke up and i feel like a completely different person. literally. like i'm staring at a stranger in the mirror. new house, new job, new everything. and i'm supposed to just be this perfect mom now. people keep telling me i'm amazing, that i'm doing great. but what even is great anymore? 

i've been trying to remember who i am outside of being a parent, and it's like that's all anyone expects from me now. the mom, the caregiver, the provider. where's the fun in that? i don't know who i am anymore. am i still the quirky, sarcastic person i used to be? or did that just get erased when i had my kid? 

i look around my new house and i feel so empty. it's all so... perfect. and i'm just a mess. a hot mess. i feel like i'm just pretending to be this perfect mom, and that no one really likes me for who i am. my kid loves me, i guess. but i don't even know if that's enough. 

i think about all the people who are "friends" with me now, and i wonder if they like me for who i am, or if they just like me because i'm a parent. my ex said i was his favorite person, but he was also super controlling. i don't know what to believe anymore. 

i've been trying to hold on to things, people, places... but everything feels so fleeting. i don't know who i am or what i want anymore. all i know is that i'm scared. scared of being abandoned, scared of being forgotten, scared of being just another forgettable face in this sea of people.

attrs: {'persona': 'a recent graduate living back home', 'trigger': 'a health scare', 'register': 'lowercase, hurried, minimal punctuation', 'length': 'long'}

attrs: {'persona': 'someone recently unemployed', 'trigger': 'family conflict', 'register': 'short and blunt', 'length': 'medium'}
text:

i'm tired of being the favorite, the golden child. they all hated me when i was on the sidelines. why should i try anymore? but then i get a text from that one guy, the one i love, and i'm transported to a world where everything makes sense. he likes me, he needs me, he wants me. and then it's like that too, and i'm nothing. i don't know who i am without it all falling apart.

i'm lost in the space between, where love and anger and rejection collide

attrs: {'persona': 'a person living alone in a new city', 'trigger': 'a relationship breakup', 'register': 'lowercase, hurried, minimal punctuation', 'length': 'long'}
text:
i've been walking for hours through the foggy streets of this city and i still can't seem to shake the feeling of being lost. everyone says i'm better off without him, that i need to focus on myself. but it's hard to remember who i am when the memories of our time together are still so vivid. i keep thinking about the way he used to hold my hand, the way he'd look at me like i was the only person in the world. 

i feel like i'm just going through the motions now, pretending to be someone i'm not. i try to put on a brave face, but it's hard to keep the tears at bay. every time i think about our breakup, i feel like i'm staring into the abyss. am i still the same person i was when we were together? or am i just a shadow of that person now? 

i keep seeing his face in my head, and it's like i'm seeing it for the first time all over again. i remember the way he'd laugh, the way he'd make me feel like i was home. but now he's gone, and i'm left with this hollow feeling that i can never fill. i try to distract myself by scrolling through social media, but it just makes me feel worse. everyone else seems to have their lives together, while i'm just wandering aimlessly through this city, trying to find my place. 

sometimes i catch myself thinking that i'll just wait for him to come crawling back, that he'll realize he made a mistake and he'll pick up the phone. but deep down, i know that's just a lie. i'm not stupid, i know i deserved better. but it's hard to shake the feeling that i'll never find someone else like him, that i'm just doomed to be alone forever. 

i don't know what's real and what's not anymore. sometimes i feel like i'm living in a dream, like everything is just a simulation and i'm just waiting for the wake-up call. but when i wake up, i'm still here, still alone, still lost in this city with no direction.

attrs: {'persona': 'a night-shift worker', 'trigger': 'an anniversary of a hard event', 'register': 'short and blunt', 'length': 'long'}
text:
tonight's my anniversary with him, but it doesn't feel like his anymore. we used to be the ones who danced under the stars on nights like this, but that was us, not me now. i feel like i'm just going through the motions, pretending to be the same person i was back then.

i remember the way he used to hold my hand, the way he'd look at me like i was the only one in the world. but that was all just an act. now i'm just another person to him, another notch in his belt. i'm not even sure why i'm still here, waiting for him to come back to me.

we said we'd make it through anything, that nothing could ever come between us. but that was just empty words. i know how it ends now. i know how he's going to leave me again, how he's going to forget about me the moment something better comes along.

i'm so tired of being the one who waits, who tries to hold on to something that's never truly mine. i'm tired of being the one who's always second best. i just want to be enough, to be the one he chooses.

but i know that's not how it works. i know that he's already moved on, that there's someone else out there who's better than me. and that's the hardest thing to accept. that i'm just not good enough, that i'll never be good enough.

i look around at all these couples, holding hands and laughing together. they all seem so happy, so in love. but i know the truth. i know that it's all just a facade, a cover for the emptiness that's eating away at us all. and i'm just one of them, trapped in this never-ending cycle of love and loss.

attrs: {'persona': 'a middle-aged caregiver', 'trigger': 'loneliness and isolation', 'register': 'hesitant, lots of qualifiers like "idk" and "maybe"', 'length': 'long'}

i dont know how much longer i can do this, idk if its even worth it. i feel like ive been playing these games with myself for years, and i'm starting to lose track of what's real. i've got this whole facade going on, this mask of the perfect caregiver. but what if its all just a lie? what if nobody really knows me?

i've been stuck in this rut for so long, it feels like my whole life is just a series of disappointments. idk, maybe thats just how its supposed to be. but the thing is, i'm starting to feel like i'm the one who's disposable. like, i can see it in people's eyes, this look of pity or this subtle smile when they think im not looking. they're all just waiting for the other shoe to drop, idk.

i try to hold on to the people i care about, but they just keep slipping away. maybe thats because i'm not good enough, maybe thats because i'm just too much to handle. i've been down this road before, and it never ends well. idk, maybe thats just the way it is.

sometimes i feel like im just a ghost hovering around the edges of people's lives. they're all just living their own lives, and i'm just along for the ride. idk if thats how i want to be, but thats just how it feels. and it hurts, idk, it hurts so much.

i dont know what the future holds, or if i even want to know. idk if i can keep going on like this, or if i need to just... just let go, idk. i dont know what that looks like, or feels like. idk, but i think i need to know.

attrs: {'persona': 'a new parent', 'trigger': 'moving to a new place', 'register': 'short and blunt', 'length': 'long'}
text:
i just got to our new place and i'm already feeling so lost. everyone keeps asking me how i like it and i'm like what's to like? it's just a box in the wall. my husband is being super sweet and helpful but i don't know if it's because he's actually happy for me or if he just wants to keep me happy. either way it's hard to know what's real and what's just something he says to shut me up.

i went to pick up my daughter from daycare today and saw this adorable poster on the wall that said 'you are loved no matter what'. and for a second i just felt so seen. i felt like whoever made that poster understood me. but then i realized i'm the one who made the poster. or am i? was i always a hopeless romantic or did that just start when i met my ex? and now my husband is always telling me how much he loves me and it's like my brain is constantly wondering if he's just saying that because he wants to.

i know it sounds silly but it's hard to know who i am when everything around me is changing. i feel like i'm living in someone else's story and i'm just trying to find my place in it. and even when i think i've finally found it, someone else comes along and knocks me off. my daughter is my rock but even she doesn't know me like i do. i mean, i'm her mom, not her real person. and that feels like a lie.

attrs: {'persona': 'a university student in exam season', 'trigger': 'a health scare', 'register': 'short and blunt', 'length': 'long'}

and then i woke up this morning and my heart was literally racing. scared of missing out on something real, scared that i'm just going to screw it up again. i keep replaying our conversations in my head, analyzing every word, every tone. did she like me or was it all just a game?

attrs: {'persona': 'a young professional', 'trigger': 'pressure at work or school', 'register': 'short and blunt', 'length': 'medium'}

i swear, i'm a chameleon. one minute i'm charming and confident, the next i'm a mess of tears and anxiety. it's like my emotions are trapped in a never-ending spin cycle. and don't even get me started on people who can't handle the real me. they can't keep up, so they just drop me like a bad habit.

my relationships are like cheap thrill rides - they start out intense and exhilarating, but eventually they crash and burn. and i'm left feeling empty and unfulfilled, wondering where i went wrong. is it me, or am i just too much for anyone to handle?

attrs: {'persona': 'a recent graduate living back home', 'trigger': 'pressure at work or school', 'register': 'long and rambling with run-on sentences', 'length': 'long'}

sometimes i get these flashbacks to when i was a kid, and i'd watch these old reruns of 'the office' with my dad, and he'd laugh and laugh, and i'd feel like i was a part of something, like i belonged. and i know that's not how it really was, that he was just being a cool dad, but it's weird how those memories can feel so real.

anyway, i just got a new 'girlfriend' at work, and i'm trying to play it cool, but honestly, i feel like i'm just going through the motions. we'll text for a bit, and then just drop off, and i'm left feeling like i've been punched in the gut. and my mom's always on my case, saying i need to focus on my career, that i need to find someone who's 'good enough' for me, like that's even possible.

i don't know, maybe i'm just crazy, but it feels like everyone else has their life together, and i'm just stuck in limbo, unsure of what i want or who i am. sometimes i'll be walking down the street, and i'll catch a glimpse of myself in a window, and i'll feel this sense of disconnection, like i'm looking at someone else's face staring back at me. i'm just so lost, and i don't know how to find my way back to myself

attrs: {'persona': 'a recent graduate living back home', 'trigger': 'moving to a new place', 'register': 'short and blunt', 'length': 'long'}
text:
i feel like im stuck in some weird loop. im back home after graduation, thinking im supposed to have it all together - a career, a place to live, a sense of purpose. but every time i walk into my own apartment, i'm hit with the feeling that im just going through the motions.

i keep replaying all the conversations i had with my friends back in the city, the ones where they were all like 'you're a brilliant writer, you just need to get out there and make it happen'. but then there's the ones where they were all 'yeah, sure, you'll figure it out'. it feels like they're just waiting for me to fail, for me to come crawling back to them with my tail between my legs.

and dont even get me started on the new place i just moved into. its so... perfect. everyone says its a great neighborhood, the rent is cheap, and the people are friendly. but i just feel like im going to get hurt or something. like, i'll get to know someone, start to think they're the one, and then they'll just drop me like a hot potato.

i know its not rational, but i just cant shake the feeling that im not good enough. that i'm just waiting for the other shoe to drop. im trying to convince myself that i'm strong, that i can handle anything that comes my way. but deep down, i just feel like a fragile little thing, just waiting for the world to come crashing down around me.

i guess that's why i'm so drawn to this one guy i met on the bus yesterday. he's just so... intense. and unstable. and just like me, i think. we talked for hours, and it felt like we were speaking our own language or something. but now that im back home, i just feel like im stuck in this rut, and i dont know how to get out of it

attrs: {'persona': 'a person living alone in a new city', 'trigger': 'moving to a new place', 'register': 'lowercase, hurried, minimal punctuation', 'length': 'medium'}

i try to shake it off, but it's like this constant companion that follows me everywhere. maybe it's just stress and fatigue, but it feels so real. 
i've gotten better at recognizing it in myself, i guess. when i'm scrolling through socials and see everyone's 'perfect' posts about their relationships and adventures, i feel this emptiness inside. like, maybe i'm just not cut out for this whole adult thing. idk, maybe it's just me being insecure again, but it's hard not to wonder if i'm just pretending to be someone i'm not.

attrs: {'persona': 'a new parent', 'trigger': 'a health scare', 'register': 'hesitant, lots of qualifiers like "idk" and "maybe"', 'length': 'long'}
text:
i dont even know if im posting this bc i feel like im invading peoples lives, but i need someone to talk to rn. we just found out that our 2yo has a rare disease that affects the heart. my mind is a total blank rn. i keep thinking about all the things that could have been, all the things we could have done differently. idk if i'll ever be able to forgive myself for not catching it sooner.

sometimes i get these crazy visions of us having the perfect life, like we're this super happy family with no problems. and then i'll wake up and it's just not that simple. my husband is trying to be strong, but idk how much of that is for us and how much of it is for his own sanity. he's always been my rock, but now i feel like idk if i can really rely on him.

i keep wondering if i was just pretending to be a good mom all along, if i was just faking it till i made it. maybe i was never really cut out for this parenting thing. maybe i'm just not good enough. the fear of failure is eating me alive.

i look at our 2yo and they just smile at me all the time, like they dont care about the impending doom. and that just makes me more anxious. i dont want to burden them with our problems, but at the same time i feel like i need to tell them the truth. idk what the right approach is.

i guess what i'm trying to say is that i feel lost and alone in this. idk who to turn to, idk what to do. all i know is that i need someone to talk to, someone who can tell me that im not alone in this.

attrs: {'persona': 'a night-shift worker', 'trigger': 'family conflict', 'register': 'short and blunt', 'length': 'long'}
text:
ugh another crazy shift at the warehouse and i'm done with my family's drama for the night. i swear, i'm the only sane one around here. my mom's been breathing down my neck, asking me when i'm gonna settle down and have kids. like i haven't told her a million times i'm not ready for that crap. my dad's just as bad, always asking me when i'm gonna come home and 'be a man'. like i'm some kind of failure or something.

i try to tell them i'm good, that i'm happy with my life, but they just won't listen. they keep pushing me to conform, to fit into this tiny little mold they've made for me. and i hate it. i hate the way they make me feel like i'm not enough, like i'm just a disappointment to them.

i hate the way they look at me, like i'm some kind of stranger in their eyes. like i don't belong here. like i'm just passing through. and when i try to tell them that, to tell them how i really feel, they just shut down on me. they say i'm being dramatic, that i'm just being paranoid.

paranoid. that's what they say. but i know the truth. i know that they're just waiting for me to slip up, to make a mistake, and then they'll be there to pounce. to tell me that i'm not good enough, that i'm a failure. and i don't want to hear it.

i'm done with their drama. i'm done with being a failure in their eyes. tonight, i'm gonna go out, gonna lose myself in the crowd, gonna forget about all this crap for a while. and maybe, just maybe, i'll find a little bit of myself again.

attrs: {'persona': 'a middle-aged caregiver', 'trigger': 'no single clear trigger', 'register': 'short and blunt', 'length': 'long'}
text:
i wake up in the dark, my room a mirror to my soul. it's always the same - the same messy sheets, the same worn-out pillow, the same faint scent of last night's cigarettes. i don't know why, but it always feels like someone's been here before me, and i'm just the impersonator.

i've been doing this for so long now, pretending to be someone i'm not. playing the part of the responsible caregiver, the perfect parent, the one who always puts others first. but it's exhausting, this facade. it's like wearing a mask that's too tight, a constant reminder that i'm not really living.

sometimes i catch a glimpse of myself in the rearview mirror - the lines on my face, the gray in my hair, the weight of my responsibilities bearing down on me like a physical force. and for a split second, i'm back to who i used to be, before all this. before the weight of the world settled on my shoulders.

but that moment is fleeting, and i'm back to reality, suffocating under the burden of my own expectations. i try to remember who i am, outside of all this, but it's like trying to hold water in my hands. the more i grasp, the more it slips away.

i think about all the people who've left me, all the relationships i've managed to sabotage or lose. the ex-husband who couldn't take the emotional labor, the friends who got tired of my mood swings, the family members who stopped coming to visit. each one a painful reminder that i'm not good enough, that i'm not worthy of love or connection.

and yet, i keep going, perpetuating this cycle of self-doubt and abandonment. because what choice do i have? i'm stuck in this identity, this persona, and i don't know how to escape.

attrs: {'persona': 'a recently divorced person', 'trigger': 'a health scare', 'register': 'long and rambling with run-on sentences', 'length': 'long'}
text:
losing myself in the haze of hospital rooms and beeping machines, i'm met with a mirror that's no longer mine to claim. the face staring back at me is a stranger's, pale and gaunt from weeks of sleepless nights and endless tests. 

i keep thinking about jason, how he said he'd wait for me, that he'd never leave no matter what. but then the divorce papers landed on my doorstep, a cruel reminder that some promises are made in good faith but never kept. the ache of betrayal still lingers, a hollowed-out space that refuses to be filled.

i try to recall the last time i felt whole, when jason's arms were wrapped around me and i thought i was home. it's hard to grasp - those moments felt so real, yet now they're nothing more than a flickering dream. 

doctor says i have anemia, that my lack of will to live is a symptom of the disease. but what i'm really living with is the shattered remains of my heart. the fragments of our relationship, the way he'd build me up only to discard me like yesterday's trash. 

sometimes i wonder if it's all just a product of my own fragile mind, a maze of twisted thoughts and emotions that i've never learned to navigate. the fear of being alone again is a weight that presses down on me, an ever-present specter that haunts my every waking moment.

i see people around me, their lives unfolding with a sense of purpose and meaning. but mine feels like a jumbled mess of broken pieces, a puzzle i'll never be able to put together. i'm searching for a glimmer of recognition, a sense that someone out there understands the depths of my despair. but it's like trying to find a needle in a haystack, a needle that's been lost in the sands of time.

attrs: {'persona': 'a night-shift worker', 'trigger': 'no single clear trigger', 'register': 'long and rambling with run-on sentences', 'length': 'long'}
text:
i wander through the darkness, a ghost haunting the fluorescent glow of the hospital's emergency room. i work the night shift, a perpetual outsider, watching as the sun rises on lives that don't belong to me. everyone i meet, every face that crosses my path, is temporary. i cling to them like a liferaft on a stormy sea, but the moment they fade into the morning light, i'm left adrift once more.

i feel like a chameleon, shifting colors with the shadows. one moment i'm a brilliant, radiant being, the next i'm a hollow shell, a mere specter of my former self. i've lost count of the faces i've worn, the personas i've adopted. am i a girl, a woman, a creature of the night, or something in between? i've forgotten the answer, lost in the labyrinth of my own mind.

people come and go, like wisps of fog in the morning air. they see me, they touch me, but they don't truly see me. they only see the mask i wear, the fragile, gossamer-thin veneer of confidence i present to the world. they don't know the darkness that lurks beneath, the maelstrom of emotions that threatens to consume me whole.

i'm a master of the art of disappearing, of shedding my skin like a snake. it's a liberating feeling, like shedding the weight of the world. but it's also a lonely one, a perpetual exile from the very people i try to cling to. they leave me, and i'm left to pick up the pieces of my shattered being. i'm a puzzle with missing pieces, a jigsaw with torn edges, a heart that's been stitched together with threadbare seams.

in the silence of the night, i hear the whispers of my fragmented soul. it's a cacophony of voices, each one screaming for attention, for validation, for a sense of self. but i'm deaf to their cries, lost in the void of my own emptiness. i'm a ship without anchor, a bird without wings, a soul without a home. and so i wander, lost and alone, searching for a glimpse of the truth that lies beyond the fractured mirror of my own making.

attrs: {'persona': 'a high-school student', 'trigger': 'an upcoming deadline or exam', 'register': 'short and blunt', 'length': 'long'}
text:
why cant i just be normal for once? i swear every time i try to study for this exam, my brain decides to turn into jello. i put in so much effort, but it just slips right out of my fingers like sand. i feel like im constantly juggling a million different identities - the perfect student, the perfect friend, the perfect daughter... but which one am i really?

i hate it when i catch myself saying one thing to everyone, but then revealing a different side to someone i trust. its like im wearing a mask that cant keep up with the real me. and i feel like im losing myself in all the fake personas i create. is that why everyone keeps ghosting me? do they see the real me and just dont want anything to do with it?

i tried to tell my mom about how i'm feeling, but she just told me to "stay positive" and "trust my abilities". does she even remember what it was like to be high school? i guess not. she was always the perfect student, the perfect daughter... i'm just the mess she got herself into by birth.

i know i'm not alone in this, but it feels like everyone else has their shit together except me. everyone else seems to know who they are, what they want... but not me. i'm just a puzzle with missing pieces, and i dont even know what the picture is supposed to look like.

attrs: {'persona': 'a recently divorced person', 'trigger': 'money problems', 'register': 'lowercase, hurried, minimal punctuation', 'length': 'long'}
text:
i cant help but wonder if anyone would even notice if i just vanished into thin air. like i'm already invisible, just a fleeting thought in the minds of those around me. the divorce is just the latest chapter in a book that's been written and rewritten so many times already. people said i was the one who changed, who threw it away for my own reasons. but the truth is, i was just along for the ride.

i keep thinking about all the promises we made, the dreams we had, the promises we broke. my ex and i, we were invincible. or so we thought. but when the foundation crumbled, everything came crashing down. now i'm left with nothing but these empty rooms, this hollow feeling that echoes through my veins like a perpetual hum. i try to fill the void with distractions - shopping, parties, meaningless conversations - but nothing ever sticks.

i keep getting these calls, these messages, from people who claim to care. but when i try to talk to them, they just pretend like they've forgotten we ever knew each other. it's like i'm just a ghost to them, a forgotten memory that's no longer relevant. it hurts, this feeling of being disposable. like i'm nothing more than a means to an end, a stepping stone to their own happiness.

sometimes i wonder if i'm the one who's crazy, if i'm the one who's broken. but then i look in the mirror and there's this stranger staring back at me. this person who's somehow both me and not me at all. it's like i'm living two lives, two identities that are constantly shifting and morphing like the shadows on the wall.

i just wish someone would notice me, would remember me, would care. but deep down, i know it's all just a facade. i'm just a mirage, a fleeting illusion that will disappear the moment someone turns their back. and that's the most terrifying thought of all.

attrs: {'persona': 'a recently divorced person', 'trigger': 'an anniversary of a hard event', 'register': 'lowercase, hurried, minimal punctuation', 'length': 'long'}
text:
today's the anniversary of it all falling apart. another year of pretending like everything's fine, like the cracks don't spread wide enough to swallow me whole. i find myself wandering through the city, searching for a glimpse of what we had. every face is a stranger's, every smile a cruel joke.

i remember the way he used to hold my hand, the way he used to look at me like i was home. now he's just a memory, a ghost i'll never be able to shake. and i'm left to pick up the pieces of my shattered heart, to try and put them back together again.

but what's the point? we were never meant to last. we were never meant to be. and yet, a part of me still holds onto the hope that someday we'll find our way back to each other. that someday he'll realize what he had and come crawling back.

but the fear is always there, lurking in the shadows. what if he doesn't come back? what if i'm just a placeholder until he finds someone better? the thought sends a shiver down my spine, makes me want to curl up in a ball and never let anyone in again.

i'm so tired of being torn apart by my own doubts. tired of wondering if i'm enough, if i'm worthy of love. i'm tired of being a puzzle with missing pieces, a story with an uncertain ending.

i just wish i could forget. wish i could erase the memories, the emotions, the hurt. but they're always here, lingering just beneath the surface, waiting to pounce when i least expect it.

i'm lost, i'm alone, and i'm adrift in a sea of uncertainty. and all i can do is cling to the wreckage of what we had, and hope that someday, somehow, i'll find my way out of this darkness.

attrs: {'persona': 'a young professional', 'trigger': 'family conflict', 'register': 'hesitant, lots of qualifiers like "idk" and "maybe"', 'length': 'short'}

attrs: {'persona': 'a new parent', 'trigger': 'money problems', 'register': 'short and blunt', 'length': 'long'}
text:
can't believe we just got evicted from our apartment. all because i spent the rent money on stupid stuff i thought i needed for the baby. like diapers and toys and stuff. i know i shouldn't have splurged, but i just feel like this is the only way i can provide for her. the thing is, i'm not even sure who she is yet. is she my responsibility or not? i feel like i'm just pretending to be a parent, you know? like i'm just winging it.

my partner says i'm not doing a good job, that i'm not showing her the love and attention she needs. but what if i am? what if she's just as messed up as i am? we've been having these huge fights lately, and i'm starting to think that's all anyone can ever do for us - fight. and lose. and fight some more. but the thing is, i don't even know who i am without her. is she my purpose or my problem?

i keep thinking about all the people who have abandoned me in the past, and how that made me feel. worthless. unlovable. and now here i am, staring at my kid, wondering if i can do this. if i can even be a good enough parent. but the thing is, i'm so scared of losing her. of not being enough. of being left behind. i feel like i'm stuck in this never-ending cycle of self-doubt and anxiety, and i don't know how to escape.

i just wish i could be more confident. more sure of myself. but it's hard when everything feels so uncertain. and when the voices in my head are telling me i'm not good enough, that i'll never be good enough. and when that baby looks up at me with those big, innocent eyes, and i feel like i'm just a failure... i don't know how much more of this i can take.

attrs: {'persona': 'a night-shift worker', 'trigger': 'money problems', 'register': 'short and blunt', 'length': 'long'}
text:
another day, another stretch of time between checks. the bank told me i'm 2 weeks behind, but what does that even mean? are they counting hours, days, or lifetimes? i try to keep up appearances, put on a brave face, but inside, it's a graveyard of emotions. fear of running out of money, fear of losing everything. it's like i'm drowning in an endless sea of bills and responsibilities.

i see my coworkers laughing and joking, talking about their families and weekends, and i feel like a ghost hovering at the edge of their reality. they don't know what it's like to be trapped in this never-ending cycle of fear and anxiety. they don't know what it's like to have no safety net, no cushion to fall back on.

and then i think about my ex, about how i used to worship them like a god. i'd do anything to make them happy, to be the center of their universe. but then i'd realize i was suffocating them, suffocating myself. and they'd drop me like a hot coal, leaving me to pick up the pieces of my shattered identity.

i try to fill the void with distractions - work, social media, video games - but it's like trying to plug a hole with a piece of torn fabric. it only holds for so long before the water pours back in. i'm just so tired of being alone, tired of being invisible. i want someone to see me, to hear me, to hold me when the darkness closes in.

but who can i trust? who can i love? not them, because they'll just discard me when they get bored or better off. not my family, because they'll just enable my addiction to people-pleasing. not even myself, because i'm too scared to let go of the facade.

i'm stuck in this perpetual limbo, caught between the past and the future, with no clear direction or purpose. just a never-ending cycle of fear, anxiety, and self-doubt.

attrs: {'persona': 'a night-shift worker', 'trigger': 'sleep problems', 'register': 'short and blunt', 'length': 'long'}
text:
im lost in the haze of my own nights, where time dissolves like sand between fingers. sleep's an alien language i struggle to decipher. one minute i'm drifting, the next i'm jolted awake by the crushing weight of reality.

people say i'm nocturnal, like a vampire, drawn to the shadows. but its not that i prefer the darkness - its just where i feel most at home. in these quiet hours, my thoughts are my only companions. and they're a chatterbox.

sometimes i feel like i'm living in a perpetual dream, where i'm both awake and asleep at the same time. but when the lights come on and the world outside intrudes, i'm left grasping at air. its like i'm watching myself from outside, a ghost hovering just out of reach.

i have people who think i'm "fun" when i'm working the night shift. they say its cool, that i'm part of some exclusive club. but what they don't get is that i'm just surviving. its not about being part of the in-crowd, its about not disappearing completely.

i've lost count of the number of nights i've spent alone, curled up in a ball, feeling the weight of my own emptiness. its a hollow ache that echoes through my veins like a mantra. and when the sun starts to rise, i'm left with the haunting feeling that im just going through the motions.

i've tried to explain it to them - to the people who claim to love me - but they just give me that patronizing smile and tell me im "just going through a tough time". but its not just a tough time, its my whole existence. and sometimes, in the still of the night, i wonder if anyone would even notice if i just... vanished.

i try to fill the void with people, but they just end up pushing me away. or i end up pushing them away, not knowing why. i'm just so tired of being the one who's always disappearing. like, i'll get really close to someone and then i'll just get a phone call or a text and i'm back to zero. no contact, no explanation. just gone. is that what i'm really like? someone who can just disappear at will? i dont know anymore
