attrs: {'persona': 'a recently divorced person', 'trigger': 'a relationship breakup', 'register': 'hesitant, lots of qualifiers like "idk" and "maybe"', 'length': 'long'}
text:
idk if i'm just being dramatic, but every time i see my ex's name pop up on my socials, i feel this gnawing sense of emptiness. like, maybe it's normal to feel this way after a breakup, but honestly, it feels like a part of me is just... unraveling.

i keep replaying our last conversation in my head, trying to pinpoint exactly what went wrong. idk, maybe it was just a moment of weakness on my part, but it feels like i said some really hurtful things, and now i'm left wondering if it was all just a big facade.

sometimes i'll catch myself idealizing the way we were together - like, remember when we used to laugh together at the same memes and stuff? but then i'll think about how we'd fight over the smallest things, and it's just... ugh. idk, maybe that's just the way relationships go, but it feels like mine was this big, messy rollercoaster.

i know i said i needed space, but sometimes it feels like i'm just pushing you away. idk, maybe that's what you wanted all along. maybe you were tired of me and just needed a clean slate. it's hard not to wonder if i was just a temporary thing in your life.

i keep telling myself to move on, that i'm better off without you, but honestly, it's hard to shake the feeling that i'm just a chapter in your life, and now that chapter is closed. idk, maybe that's just the way it goes sometimes, but it feels like i'm still stuck in the past, reliving the what-ifs and the maybes.

i guess what i'm trying to say is, idk, maybe i'm just not ready to move on yet. maybe i need more time to figure out who i am without you. but a part of me is scared that i'll never find my way again, that i'll just be lost in this endless sea of uncertainty, trying to find a new identity that doesn't revolve around you. idk, maybe that's just the way it goes sometimes, but it feels like i'm drowning in my own insecurities.

attrs: {'persona': 'a high-school student', 'trigger': 'a health scare', 'register': 'hesitant, lots of qualifiers like "idk" and "maybe"', 'length': 'long'}
text:
idk what's going on with me rn, but after that hospital visit my mom told me about, i'm freaking out. they said something about me being in a 'gray area' between anxiety and depression, but honestly, idk what that even means. maybe i'm just anxious, maybe i'm depressed, maybe i'm just lost.

ever since that test showed my potassium levels were off, i've been having these weird feelings of emptiness. it's like, i'm supposed to be 'fine' and 'healthy' but nothing feels right. my friends all seem so... on top of things, you know? they're all busy with school and stuff, but they still manage to look so put together. me? idk, i just feel like i'm floating through life on autopilot.

i keep thinking about that guy i met at the hospital, though. he's in a wheelchair and he's, like, super into music. we talked for a bit, and i swear, he was one of the only people who seemed to actually get me. he didn't try to fix me or tell me to 'be positive', he just listened. and i felt seen, you know?

but, of course, my mom freaked out when she found out about him. she said i need to focus on my own healing and not get distracted by people who might 'hurt' me. and, idk, maybe she's right. maybe i am just too sensitive. but it's hard not to wonder if that guy felt the same way i do - like, we connected on this deep level, but it was all so fleeting.

sometimes i feel like i'm just a ghost drifting through people's lives, invisible and insignificant. and that's what's scaring me. not the hospital visit or the test results, but the feeling that i'm just going to disappear into nothingness. idk, maybe i'm just being dramatic, but it feels like that's what's happening.

text:
tonight's the day that's supposed to be all about him - another anniversary of the day he left me, but it's just another reminder of the 47 others since then. i know he says he loves me, but his actions speak louder. he'll cling to me for months, whispering sweet nothings in my ear, telling me i'm the only one for him. but as soon as i start to relax, he's gone, vanished into thin air like a ghost. i've lost count of the number of times i've had to pick up the pieces of my shattered heart.

the thing is, i don't even know who he is anymore. or who we are, really. we're just two people who can't seem to make up our minds about each other. sometimes i feel like i'm the one who's meant to be loved, but other times i wonder if i'm just a placeholder until he gets bored and moves on to someone else. it's a never-ending cycle of highs and lows, and i'm tired of being stuck in it.

i keep thinking that this time will be different, that this anniversary will mark a turning point. but history repeats itself, and i'm not naive enough to think otherwise. i've been here before, and i know how it ends. still, a part of me holds on to the hope that maybe, just maybe, this time will be different. that maybe he'll actually put in the effort to make it work, to show me that he's committed. but deep down, i know it's just a futile hope. tonight, as i sit here staring at the clock, waiting for the inevitable, all i can think is - when will i be enough for him? when will i be enough for myself?

attrs: {'persona': 'a night-shift worker', 'trigger': 'money problems', 'register': 'long and rambling with run-on sentences', 'length': 'long'}
text:
i'm drowning in the monotony of this shift, the fluorescent lights above me mirroring the numbness that's taken up residence in my chest. another day, another dollar, another excuse to push through the exhaustion that threatens to consume me whole. but it's not just the work that's suffocating me, it's the weight of the world that's sitting on my shoulders, waiting for me to crack.

i swear to myself that i'll be better, that i'll make ends meet, that i'll find a way to escape this cycle of debt and desperation. but every time i think i'm making progress, something comes along and threatens to unravel everything. like that guy i met a few weeks ago, the one who promised me the world but turned out to be nothing more than a mirage on the horizon. or the girl i dated last year, who seemed perfect but was actually just using me for her own validation.

i'm starting to think that i'm just a puzzle piece that doesn't fit anywhere, that i'm constantly searching for the right shape to fit into but never quite finding it. my relationships are like a game of Jenga, where one wrong move and the whole thing comes crashing down. and i'm the one who's always reaching for the piece that'll make it all fit together, but it's like trying to solve a riddle that's been written in invisible ink.

sometimes i wonder if it's all just a big show, if everyone's just playing their part in this twisted game we call life. am i just a prop, a pawn in someone else's game of cat and mouse? or am i the one holding the knife, the one who's pulling the strings? i don't know anymore, all i know is that i'm lost in the darkness, searching for a glimpse of light in the abyss.

text:

i keep replaying the last time we hung out, wondering what i did wrong. was it something i said? no, she just laughed it off and said im funny. but then again, why did she let me stay at her place till 3 am talking about our future? did i just give her a blank check?

i swear i was her everything. i was her rock. or so i thought. she was mine. or so i thought. now i'm left with just a hollow feeling, like i was just a stop on her way to somewhere else.

i try to remember what she liked, what made her tick. but it's all just a blur. was it the way i listened? the way i made her laugh? the way i held her hand? or was it just a facade? a convenient story i told myself to keep her close?

i know i'm not worthy of anyone. i know i'll just get hurt again. but what choice do i have? i'll just keep searching for someone who can see past my crap. past my insecurities. past my stupid mistakes. but what if she sees the real me? what if she gets tired of pretending?

i don't know what i want anymore. i don't know what i need. all i know is that i'm lost, and i'm scared, and i'm just trying to hold on to something, anything, that feels like home.

text:
tonight's the anniversary of the shift that changed everything. been thinking about that night a lot lately, wondering how i ended up stuck in this cycle of desperation and hope. used to be, i thought i was some kind of superhero, always saving others but never saving myself. but that was before the crash, before the universe decided to remind me that even the strongest among us can fall.

i remember the people i used to love, the ones i used to think i could trust. there was alex, the one who always knew how to make me laugh, who always knew how to make me feel like i was home. and then there was jess, the one who saw right through me, who knew how to push my buttons and make me question everything. both of them are gone now, or so i thought.

the thing is, i'm still here, still working this same shift, still dealing with the same demons. and it's like, what's the point? is anyone even listening? do i even matter? the emptiness is suffocating, a constant reminder that i'm just a tiny speck in an infinite universe.

i try to fill the void with work, with distractions, with people, but it never lasts. they always leave, or they always let me down. and i'm left here, alone with my thoughts, wondering what's wrong with me. am i broken? am i unlovable? or is this just the way the world works?

i don't know anymore. all i know is that i'm tired, so tired of being tired. tired of being alone. tired of being me. but what's the alternative? who am i supposed to be? and why can't i just be happy for once?

it's not like i'm asking for much. just a little bit of connection, a little bit of understanding. but is that too much to ask? or am i just deluding myself, expecting something that's never going to happen.

attrs: {'persona': 'a night-shift worker', 'trigger': 'an upcoming deadline or exam', 'register': 'long and rambling with run-on sentences', 'length': 'long'}
text:
another sleepless night ahead of me, the clock on my wall ticking away like a death sentence. the hours blend together, an endless sea of darkness punctuated only by the occasional beep of my computer. i try to focus, but my mind keeps drifting to the looming deadline, the exam that's going to make or break my career. i've been preparing for this moment for what feels like an eternity, but now that it's finally here, i'm paralyzed with fear.

my thoughts are a jumbled mess, jumping from worst-case scenarios to visions of success, each one fleeting and incomprehensible. i feel like i'm stuck in this perpetual state of limbo, unable to move forward or backward. my sense of identity is a fragile, flickering flame that threatens to extinguish itself at any moment. who am i, really? what kind of person do i want to be? the questions swirl in my head like a maelstrom, impossible to untangle.

i look around my cluttered apartment, the familiar comforts of home a reminder that i'm still here, still alive. but what if i fail? what if i'm not good enough? the thought sends a shiver down my spine, a creeping sense of dread that seeps into my bones. i try to push it away, to focus on the task at hand, but it's like trying to hold water in my hands - the harder i squeeze, the more it slips away.

i think back to all the people who've been there for me over the years, the ones who've supported me and encouraged me. but even they can be so unpredictable, so prone to abandoning me when it's convenient for them. it's like i'm stuck in this never-ending cycle of idealization and rejection, never quite sure who to trust. and then there's the fear of abandonment itself, the constant terror that i'll be left behind, left to face the world alone.

the more i think about it, the more i realize that i'm not just afraid of failure - i'm afraid of being forgotten. of being left behind. of disappearing into the void, never to be seen or heard from again. it's a terrifying prospect, one that keeps me up at night, my heart racing with anxiety. and so i sit here, in the darkness, trying to find the courage to face whatever comes next. but for now, i'm stuck, frozen in this limbo of uncertainty.

attrs: {'persona': 'a new parent', 'trigger': 'an upcoming deadline or exam', 'register': 'short and blunt', 'length': 'long'}
text:
i'm 3 days away from my final exam and all i can think about is that my baby is growing up way too fast. it feels like yesterday we were bringing her home from the hospital, helpless and dependent on us. now she's giggling and babbling like she's 5, pulling her own hair, and refusing to nap. i love it, but a part of me is terrified that i'll lose her to the world before she's even born into it.

my friends all seem to have their lives together - the career, the relationships, the perfect little homes. but what's the point of it all if my little girl is the only one who truly sees me? she doesn't care about my grades or my deadlines, she just loves me for who i am, messy and imperfect. and that's what keeps me going, but it also makes me feel so utterly unworthy.

i look around at all the moms at school and i feel like an imposter. they all have their little crew of friends and their family events, their perfect little lives. and me? i'm just a solo parent, trying to keep my head above water while my daughter is still learning to walk. it's hard not to feel like i'm failing her, like i'm not good enough.

i try to remind myself that i'm doing okay, that i'm making it through each day and providing a loving home for my child. but the voices in my head are always whispering, telling me that i'm not enough. that i'm just holding on by a thread, waiting for the other shoe to drop. and when it does, i'll be left standing alone, my daughter gone off to college and me left to pick up the pieces.

i guess what i'm trying to say is that being a parent is the most terrifying thing i've ever done. but it's also the most beautiful thing. it's like, i'm scared of losing her, but i'm also scared of growing up without her by my side. and that's what keeps me up at night, wondering if i'm doing enough to prepare for the future, and if i'll be able to let her go when the time comes.

attrs: {'persona': 'a recently divorced person', 'trigger': 'a health scare', 'register': 'hesitant, lots of qualifiers like "idk" and "maybe"', 'length': 'long'}
text:
ugh, idk how i'm even gonna start this, but i guess i need to talk to someone about this. so, i just got back from the doctor and they told me i have a minor heart condition. like, not a big deal or anything, but it's still kinda freaking me out. my ex was supposed to call me today to check in, but they haven't said anything. i'm trying not to read too much into it, but a part of me is just feeling super anxious and abandoned. like, maybe they just forgot or something, but deep down, i know that's not it.

anyway, this doctor's visit got me thinking about my own sense of self. idk if i'm just being dramatic, but i feel like i've been living in this perpetual state of limbo since my divorce. like, i'm not really sure who i am without them. maybe i was just a facade, a persona i created to keep them happy. now that it's all gone, i'm left wondering if i even exist.

i've been trying to fill the void with work and hobbies, but it's just not the same. i feel like i'm just going through the motions, playing the part of the "strong, independent person". but sometimes, in the quiet moments, i catch glimpses of this other person, the one who's lost and scared and unsure of what to do. maybe that's the real me, maybe that's just my vulnerable side coming out. idk, it's all so confusing.

i guess what i'm trying to say is that i feel like i'm just trying to put together a puzzle with missing pieces. i'm trying to figure out who i am, what i want, and where i'm going, but it's like the more i learn, the more i realize how little i know. it's scary, but it's also kinda exhilarating, you know? like, maybe this is the start of something new, maybe this is my chance to create a new identity, one that's not based on anyone else's expectations.

but what if i fail? what if i'm just pretending to be someone i'm not? what if i'm just a pretend person, living a pretend life? idk, maybe that's just the existential crisis talking, but it's definitely on my mind right now.

attrs: {'persona': 'a high-school student', 'trigger': 'an anniversary of a hard event', 'register': 'lowercase, hurried, minimal punctuation', 'length': 'long'}
text:
today's the anniversary of that night i lost everything. literally. my entire life seemed to unravel before my eyes. the fire at my parents' old apartment, it was like the world was ending. and in a way, it was. the flames consumed not just our home, but our whole relationship. my mom and dad were fighting for weeks before it all went up in flames. i knew then that it was the end of us, of them, of everything we thought we had.

i'm sitting here now, looking at old photos of us, and it's like i'm staring at a stranger. their smile, my smile - we're so different now. i feel like i've been living a lie, playing a role, pretending to be someone i'm not. and it's suffocating me.

i keep thinking about all the other times, the other relationships that have fizzled out. my friends from middle school, we were inseparable, and then they grew up, moved away, and forgot about me. i'm left with this emptiness, this hollowness that i don't know how to fill. it's like i'm constantly searching for validation, for someone to love me for who i am, but it's like i'm always chasing a ghost.

i look at my phone, and it's full of texts from people i've met online, people who seem to understand me, who get me. but i know it's just a facade, a mask. they don't really know me, not like i think they do. and when they do, when they finally see the real me, they'll leave me too.

i don't know how much more of this i can take. the constant fear of abandonment, the fear of being left behind. it's like i'm trapped in this never-ending cycle, and i don't know how to escape. all i know is that i need to keep moving, keep searching for that elusive sense of belonging. but for now, i'm stuck, lost in this abyss, trying to find a mirror in the darkest recesses of my soul

text:
ok, i dont know how much more of this i can take, i swear im just not cut out for this whole social thing anymore. idk, maybe its just me, but every time i have to go to a gathering or a party, i just feel like i'm going to melt down or something. and its not even like i'm avoiding it or anything, im actually a pretty friendly person, but its just that i feel like i'm always on, you know? like i have to be 'on' and present and charming all the time, and it just takes a lot out of me.

and its not just the actual events themselves, its the planning and the prep that really gets to me. trying to come up with conversation topics that are interesting but not too personal, or finding out who's going to be there so i can avoid, like, awkward small talk with people i barely know... idk, it just feels like so much work. and sometimes i feel like im not even doing it right, like i'm just phoning it in or something.

and the worst part is, i feel like i'm not doing this for myself, i'm just doing it because i feel like i have to. because my family expects it of me, or because im supposed to be this certain kind of person. but honestly, the thought of having to put on that mask and be all cheerful and friendly for just one more minute is just so exhausting.

idk, maybe im just being ridiculous, maybe im just being a scared old person, but i feel like im just so overwhelmed by all of this. and i dont know how to make it stop. i feel like im just going to keep going on like this until i just...burn out or something.

attrs: {'persona': 'a recently divorced person', 'trigger': 'no single clear trigger', 'register': 'hesitant, lots of qualifiers like "idk" and "maybe"', 'length': 'long'}
text:
idk how much more of this i can take rn... been 6 months since the divorce and i'm still trying to figure out who i am without my partner. it feels like every aspect of my life is out of whack - my job is demanding, my friends are all couples, and my family just wants me to "move on" and "find someone new". but idk, i don't feel like i'm over it yet.

the thing is, my ex and i were never really 'us' as a couple, if you know what i mean. we were just two people who happened to be in a relationship. and now that it's over, i'm realizing that we had different goals and aspirations. mine are pretty basic, maybe even boring - i just wanna live a simple life, work hard, and enjoy the little things. but my ex was all about chasing success and making a name for herself.

anyway, it's been tough finding my footing again. my boss is breathing down my neck about meeting deadlines, my social media friends are all making plans for the weekend and i'm just standing here idk what to do, and my family is always on my case about 'getting back out there'... it feels like everyone's pushing me to be someone i'm not.

maybe i'm just not cut out for this 'independent life' thing, maybe i should just settle down with someone who knows what they want. but at the same time, i don't want to give up on myself. i want to prove to myself that i can do it, that i can be happy and fulfilled on my own terms.

idk, it's all just so overwhelming right now. i feel like i'm just trying to keep my head above water and i'm not even sure how much longer i can keep going...

attrs: {'persona': 'a young professional', 'trigger': 'pressure at work or school', 'register': 'short and blunt', 'length': 'long'}
text:
i just can't catch a break. been working non stop for the past 3 months and my manager is breathing down my neck about meeting these impossible deadlines. every time i think i'm finally done with a project, another one pops up and takes priority. i'm a software engineer and i love my job, but this is ridiculous.

and to make matters worse, i just got an email from my school saying that they're going to be increasing my course load by 2 classes next semester. i already have 12 credits and i'm barely keeping my head above water. i have to take out a student loan just to pay for textbooks and i'm already worried about how i'm going to afford the rent on my apartment.

i'm feeling like i'm losing myself in all of this. i used to love coding and problem-solving, but now it just feels like a chore. i'm just trying to make ends meet and get through each day without losing my mind. my friends are all doing okay, but they seem to be passing me by. i'm starting to feel like i'm in way over my head.

i know i'm not alone, but it feels like everyone around me is moving forward and i'm just stuck in neutral. i'm worried that if i don't get my life together soon, i'll be left behind. i just wish i could have more control over my schedule and my finances. is that too much to ask?

anyway, just venting to anyone who will listen. thanks for listening

attrs: {'persona': 'a recent graduate living back home', 'trigger': 'loneliness and isolation', 'register': 'hesitant, lots of qualifiers like "idk" and "maybe"', 'length': 'long'}
text:
i don't know how much more of this i can take, maybe i'm just being dramatic, but it feels like every aspect of my life is piling on me right now. i've been back home for a few months after grad school and it's been...interesting. my parents are super nice and all, but they don't really get it, you know? they just tell me to "stay positive" and "follow my dreams" but they don't really see the reality of my situation.

i have no job, no real plan, and my parents are always pushing me to get into something "stable" which makes me feel like i'm a failure or something. i'm just trying to navigate this whole "post-grad" thing and find something that makes me happy, but it feels like everyone around me is already set and i'm just stuck.

i have friends from school still, but we all kind of drifted apart after graduation and it's hard to find common ground now. they're all moving on with their lives and getting jobs and getting married...while i'm over here trying to figure out what i even want to do with my life. it's like i'm stuck in this limbo and i don't know how to escape.

i know i'm not alone, but it feels like everyone else has their act together and i'm just a mess. i feel like i'm the only one who's struggling to find direction and purpose, and it's hard to talk to people about it because they just don't get it. i'm trying to stay positive, but it's hard when it feels like the whole world is moving on without me.

i don't know, maybe i'm just feeling really overwhelmed right now, but it feels like the pressure is piling on me and i don't know how to keep up. i just wish someone could tell me that it's okay to not have it all figured out yet, that it's okay to take my time and figure things out.

text:
idk how much more of this i can take. been on the phone with my mom nonstop trying to figure out my finances. i'm only 22 and just got a bachelor's degree in finance, but my mom says i'm not gonna make it in the job market. maybe it's true, idk. i feel like i'm stuck in limbo.

anyway, my health insurance is about to lapse and i'm freaking out. i need to get a job ASAP but the only ones available are minimum wage or i want to graduate school. i don't wanna graduate school, but i don't wanna live without health insurance either. my mom says we should just move in with her, but that would mean taking on even more responsibilities.

and to make things worse, my dad's been having some health issues lately and i feel like i'm not doing enough to help him. maybe it's just my imagination, but i swear i'm getting more and more anxious by the day. my friend and i were trying to plan a fun weekend, but i kept putting it off because i just didn't feel like it.

i know i need to get my priorities straight and start tackling these problems head-on, but idk where to start. i feel like i'm just spinning my wheels and going nowhere fast. maybe i should just admit defeat and accept that things will never get better. idk, that sounds super depressing even to me.

i guess what i'm trying to say is that i'm just really, really overwhelmed right now and i don't know how to make it all stop. i feel like i'm losing control and i don't know how to get it back.

attrs: {'persona': 'a middle-aged caregiver', 'trigger': 'loneliness and isolation', 'register': 'lowercase, hurried, minimal punctuation', 'length': 'long'}
text:
i've been taking care of my elderly mom for years now, ever since her husband passed away. i'm not even sure how old she is anymore, but the doctor says she's still in decent health for her age. my husband passed away too, a few years ago, and i've been on my own since then. my kids are grown and moved out, they're all doing well, but that just means i'm left to deal with my mom's health issues and my own loneliness.

i try to do everything myself, from grocery shopping to doctor's appointments to keeping her house clean. my days blend together in a blur of responsibilities. sometimes i feel like i'm just barely keeping my head above water. i'm exhausted all the time, but i don't dare take a break because there's always something else that needs doing.

today was one of those days. my mom had a fall in the morning and i had to take her to the hospital. the bill is huge and i'm still trying to figure out how we're going to pay it. and to make matters worse, the social worker just told me that our home is being sold because the seller needs the money and we have to be out by the end of the month. i'm supposed to start looking for a new place to live tomorrow. but how can i do that when i'm already drowning in bills and responsibilities?

i feel like i'm losing myself in all of this. i'm not even sure who i am anymore. i'm just a caregiver, a wife, a daughter, a daughter-in-law... i've lost my identity and i don't know how to get it back. sometimes i wish i had someone to talk to, someone to share this burden with. but i'm too ashamed to ask for help. so i just keep putting on a brave face and trying to keep everything together

text:
so it's been a year since we lost our baby girl, and idk how to even write about it without getting emotional. maybe it's just the anniversary, but it feels like the weight of everything is crushing me. i keep thinking i'm doing everything wrong, that i'm just pretending to be a good parent while secretly failing. it's like there's this constant voice in my head saying "what if you had done things differently?" or "you're just so lucky to have two kids alive"

i don't know how other parents do it, it feels like i'm just barely holding on. work has been a nightmare lately, with all these new demands and expectations. i'm always playing catch-up, feeling like i'm constantly falling behind. maybe i should just be grateful for what i have, but it's hard not to compare myself to others who seem to have it all together

and then there's the pressure from my partner, feeling like we need to be more involved in the kids' lives, to be more present. but what if we're not doing enough? what if we're messing it up? it feels like there's this constant sense of inadequacy, like we're just pretending to be a good family while secretly falling apart

i know i need to take a deep breath and remind myself that i'm doing my best. but it's hard when it feels like the world is watching, when every little thing seems to be a failure waiting to happen. idk, maybe i just need to trust that i'm doing okay, that i'm not alone in this. but for now, it just feels like i'm drowning in a sea of responsibilities, with no lifeline in sight.

today's anniversary is like a punch to the gut, i know i should be expecting it but it still catches me off guard, remembering the day the papers were signed and our world crumbled into a million pieces i feel so guilty for even thinking about celebrating it, like its going to bring back the pain and the tears but the truth is it's been a year and i'm still trying to pick up the shards of my shattered heart 
i woke up this morning and the pressure to get everything perfect for the kids, the house, our schedules, it's all just too much, i feel like i'm running on empty trying to keep the facade going, pretending like everything is okay when in reality it's not, i'm barely holding it together and the stress is eating away at me 
i keep thinking about all the things we used to do together, the memories we made, the laughter, the inside jokes and it feels like it's all been taken away from me, leaving me with just a hollow shell of what once was, i try to push the thoughts away but they linger, haunting me, reminding me of what i lost 
i'm so scared of not being able to provide for the kids, of not being able to be the mom they deserve, of not being able to give them the love and stability they need, it's a weight that's crushing me and i don't know how much longer i can keep it up, i feel like i'm drowning in a sea of responsibilities, with each wave bringing new tasks, new bills to pay, new responsibilities to take on, and my mind is a stormy ocean that refuses to calm, i just want to scream and cry and run away from it all, but i know i have to keep going, for the kids, for myself, for the chance to start over and maybe, just maybe, find a way to heal

attrs: {'persona': 'a recent graduate living back home', 'trigger': 'sleep problems', 'register': 'short and blunt', 'length': 'long'}
text:
I cant even begin to explain how exhausted I am right now. It's been 3 months since I graduated and I'm still trying to wrap my head around the fact that I'm still living at home. My parents are breathing down my neck for a job, but nobody's hiring in my field and I'm starting to lose hope. I feel like I'm failing them, but I don't know how to fix this situation.

School's not much better. I'm still taking classes online, but it's taking me forever to finish my degree. My coursework is intense, and I'm constantly worried that I won't be able to pass my exams. I'm trying to balance school with job searching, but it's impossible. I'm working 20 hour shifts at a part-time job just to make ends meet, but it's not enough.

And to make matters worse, my roommate just quit and I'm stuck with two roommates who are barely paying their rent on time. I'm constantly stressing about money, and I just feel like I'm drowning in all of this. I'm barely sleeping, and when I do, I wake up in the middle of the night with anxiety attacks.

I feel like I'm stuck in this rut and I don't know how to get out. I'm tired of being the one who's always struggling. I'm tired of being the one who's always worried. I just want to be able to breathe again, you know? I want to be able to go to bed at night without worrying about what's going to happen tomorrow. But for now, I'm stuck in this cycle of stress and anxiety, and I don't know how to escape.

attrs: {'persona': 'a recent graduate living back home', 'trigger': 'family conflict', 'register': 'short and blunt', 'length': 'long'}
text:
i cant even right now. my parents are on my case about my lack of job prospects and my brother is being super unsupportive. i'm trying to look for a job but its tough when they keep asking me when ill be moving back in and when ill be financially stable. i told them im doing my best but they just keep pushing me.

it feels like they never believed in me when i was in school. now that im out, they just expect me to magically get a good job and become successful. i've been applying to a ton of places but no one is even responding to my applications. my parents keep saying i need to update my resume and that i need to network more. like i dont have time for all that.

i'm so done with their constant criticism and skepticism. it feels like they think i'm just going through the motions and that i'm never actually doing anything. but what can i tell them? that i'm trying my best? that i'm struggling just like everyone else? 

my brother keeps saying that if i was really dedicated to getting a job, i would have found one already. but what if i have been looking? what if i've been applying to places that are just too far away or that don't pay enough? what if i'm just not as good as everyone else? i dont know anymore.

i just wish they would support me for once. not just tolerate my presence in their lives but actually believe in me. i feel like i'm walking on eggshells around them and i dont know how much more of this i can take.

attrs: {'persona': 'a new parent', 'trigger': 'social situations', 'register': 'short and blunt', 'length': 'long'}
text:
i just got back from the most exhausting family gathering and i'm still trying to recover. we all had to pretend to be happy and make small talk with relatives who barely know us, and i was on edge the whole time. i feel like a total fake, and my anxiety was through the roof. but it's not just the social anxiety that's got me feeling like i'm losing my mind. it's the pressure to be the perfect parent, to have all the answers, to make sure everyone is happy and healthy.

i mean, don't even get me started on trying to manage my kid's schedule. they're in that stage where they're constantly asking for things and demanding attention, and i feel like i'm drowning in a sea of requests. i'm the one who has to make sure they get to their activities on time, who has to calm them down when they're upset, who has to be the one to take care of them when they're sick. it's a lot of pressure, and sometimes i feel like i'm not doing enough.

and then to make matters worse, my partner is going through a tough time at work, and we're both exhausted. we're barely getting any sleep, and when we do, we're too tired to even enjoy it. i'm starting to feel like we're just going through the motions, like we're just existing in a state of perpetual stress. i just want to be able to have a conversation without it feeling like a negotiation, to be able to take a deep breath without feeling like i'm going to pass out.

i know i'm not alone in this feeling, but it feels like it sometimes. it feels like i'm just a single parent trying to juggle a million different balls in the air, and if i drop one, everything is going to come crashing down. i guess what i'm trying to say is that it's hard to be a functioning adult sometimes, especially when you're trying to be a good parent.

attrs: {'persona': 'someone recently unemployed', 'trigger': 'moving to a new place', 'register': 'short and blunt', 'length': 'long'}
text:
I'm not sure how much more of this I can handle. It's been three months since I lost my job and now I'm dealing with the aftermath of relocating to a new city. I thought I'd have time to find a new place, a new job, and get settled before the housing market starts to heat up again, but nope. I just got a notice that my new apartment is going to be rented out a month earlier than expected and now I'm having to scramble to find a new one. 

I'm trying to navigate this new city with my family, but it's not easy. My partner is still looking for a job, my kid is adjusting to a new school, and I'm trying to set up a home office so I can continue working remotely. The pressure to find a job is immense, and I feel like I'm juggling too many balls in the air. 

I've been applying to jobs left and right, but it's like they're all saying no. I've had a few interviews, but nothing's panned out yet. I'm starting to feel like I'm failing, like I'm not doing enough. I keep telling myself it's not my fault, that it's just a tough market, but it's hard not to take it personally. 

The worst part is that I feel like I'm letting everyone down. My family is counting on me to make ends meet, and I don't want to disappoint them. I'm worried about how they'll pay the bills, how we'll make ends meet. It's a constant anxiety that's weighing on me. I just wish I could find a job already so I could breathe a sigh of relief. But for now, I'm stuck in this limbo, feeling overwhelmed and unsure of what to do next.

attrs: {'persona': 'a middle-aged caregiver', 'trigger': 'sleep problems', 'register': 'lowercase, hurried, minimal punctuation', 'length': 'long'}
text:
i just cant seem to catch up on anything anymore. i'm working 3 jobs just to pay the bills and take care of my family, but somehow i still manage to put my own life on hold. my youngest daughter is starting kindergarten next week and i'm supposed to be all excited for her, but honestly i'm just worried about how ill be able to get her to school on time, and what if she gets sick? and dont even get me started on the laundry, dishes, and grocery shopping that pile up by the end of each day. my sleep is just so bad these days, i'm exhausted all the time and my mind is a jumble of worries about everything.

i try to remember what it was like to have a social life, or even just a quiet cup of coffee in the morning without someone needing help. but the constant demands on my time just keep piling up and im starting to feel like im just going through the motions. my oldest son is going through a phase where he refuses to go to bed at night, so i'm stuck up late changing diapers and soothing his tears. and my youngest is just starting to assert her independence, so she wants to do more and more things on her own, which just means im on the phone with the school or the vet all day.

i feel like im failing everyone, not just as a parent, but as a person. i'm exhausted, i'm irritable, and i just feel like im drowning. i try to put on a brave face for everyone's sake, but deep down im just counting down the hours until i can collapse onto the couch and just not have to think about any of it for a while. is this what it feels like to be a normal person who has their life together? because right now, it feels like i'm just barely scraping by, and i dont know how much longer im going to be able to keep going like this.

attrs: {'persona': 'a recent graduate living back home', 'trigger': 'money problems', 'register': 'long and rambling with run-on sentences', 'length': 'long'}
text:
i feel like i'm barely holding on here, like the weight of the world is crushing me and i just can't seem to find my footing. i've been back home for like two weeks now and i thought i'd have some time to breathe, to relax and recharge after all the craziness of finals. but nope, it seems like the stress just follows me everywhere.

so first of all, we just found out that my parents' car broke down and they need to get it fixed ASAP or else they'll lose their car insurance. and of course, they can't just take it to some random mechanic, no they need to go to this one guy who charges an arm and a leg. so now my mom's been stressing out about how they're gonna afford it and i'm trying to help out by taking on some extra shifts at work but honestly, it's not like i can take on that kind of financial burden by myself.

and then there's the fact that my parents are still paying off the mortgage on their place and they just got a notice from the bank that they're going to lose their home if they don't make a payment next month. so now my dad's been freaking out about how they're gonna deal with that too and i'm just trying to be supportive but it's hard when i feel like i'm not making a difference.

and to top it all off, i just got accepted into a program that i really want to be a part of but it's going to require me to take out a ton of student loans. i'm already drowning in debt from school and i don't know how i'm gonna pay it all back. i feel like i'm just being pulled in so many different directions and i don't know how to keep up.

i know i'm not alone in this, i know that lots of people are dealing with similar struggles, but it feels like the weight of the world is on my shoulders right now and i just want to scream. i wish i could just find a way to make everything better, to make all of these problems disappear. but for now, i'm just stuck feeling overwhelmed and powerless.

my job is stressing me out too. i work at the local ice cream shop and people always ask me for advice or something and it feels like they expect me to be all cheerful and stuff. like i'm some kind of happy go lucky person who got the perfect relationship. no one wants to talk to me because they know i got dumped. i feel like i'm just invisible.

and to make matters worse my family is always on my case about something. my mom wants me to get a new boyfriend like stat and my dad wants me to focus on my studies and get good grades. they have no idea what i'm going through. they just want me to "get over it" and move on. but how can i when everything is still so fresh? i just wish they could understand that relationships take work and sometimes they dont pay off.

i dont know how much more of this i can take. it feels like i'm drowning in a sea of responsibility and expectations. school, work, family, and now i'm just trying to survive the emotional fallout of a breakup. sometimes i wonder if it's all worth it.

idk how much more of this i can take. it feels like my whole world has been turned upside down since sarah and i broke up. i'm not even sure how it happened, idk, it just did. one minute we were fine, the next we were done. and now i'm just sitting here in my empty apartment, wondering what went wrong.

maybe it's just me, but i feel like i've lost myself in this whole thing. i used to be this happy, carefree person, but now i just feel numb. idk, maybe it's just the grief of losing sarah, but it feels like a part of me is missing.

i keep thinking about how easy it would be to just disappear. idk, maybe it sounds crazy, but it feels like that's exactly what i need to do. just fade away, you know? not wake up, not have to deal with any of this anymore. it's a tempting thought, idk.

i know it sounds stupid, but sometimes i find myself just zoning out, staring blankly at the wall. it's like my brain is just shutting down, idk. and when that happens, i feel this weird sense of peace. like everything is just... over.

i know i'm not supposed to think like this, idk. i know i'm supposed to be strong, to move on. but idk, maybe that's just not possible for me right now. maybe i just need to accept that some days are harder than others, and that it's okay to not be okay.

i guess what i'm trying to say is that i'm really struggling right now. idk, maybe it's just the aftermath of the breakup, but i feel like i'm just drifting through life, without any direction or purpose. and that's a really scary feeling, idk.

attrs: {'persona': 'someone recently unemployed', 'trigger': 'loneliness and isolation', 'register': 'lowercase, hurried, minimal punctuation', 'length': 'long'}
text:
i feel like i'm just a blip on the radar of society. no one knows me and i know no one. my friends are all busy with their own lives and family. i'm just a lonely ghost drifting through the days. i wake up every morning and i don't know what to do with myself. the only thing that brings me comfort is my bed. i don't know how much longer i can keep going like this.

i look around at everyone else and they all seem to be so happy. they all have their families and friends and fulfilling jobs. but for me it's just the opposite. i'm invisible. i'm just a number on a resume. a faceless drone in a sea of people. i don't know what i did wrong to deserve this. i thought i was a good person. i thought i was worthy of love and respect.

sometimes i just wish i could disappear. wish i could just fade away into nothingness. no more pain no more sadness no more loneliness. just nothing. i don't want to wake up tomorrow or the next day or the next. i don't want to face whatever it is that's waiting for me. i just want to stop.

i know it sounds ridiculous but it feels like that's the only way out. the only way to escape the crushing weight of my own existence. i just want to be free from this prison of loneliness. but i don't know how to get out. i don't know how to be seen or heard or loved. all i know is that i'm tired. i'm so, so tired of fighting. i'm tired of trying. i'm tired of being.

i just wish someone would notice me. wish someone would care. wish someone would just see me. but no one does. no one ever has. and it's just so... so... ugh. i don't even have the words to describe how i'm feeling. all i can say is that i just want to disappear. wish i could just disappear.

attrs: {'persona': 'a recently divorced person', 'trigger': 'an anniversary of a hard event', 'register': 'lowercase, hurried, minimal punctuation', 'length': 'long'}
text: today's the anniversary and i'm completely lost. it's been a year since everything fell apart and i'm still trying to figure out how to pick up the pieces of myself. i keep thinking about us and how it all ended but not just that. i keep thinking about how i'll never be whole again. i wish nothing more than to just disappear from everyone's lives. not wake up one morning and just be gone. or for everything to just stop moving. for the world to just stand still and for me to be nothing but a memory. but at the same time i'm terrified of being found. of being left behind. of being the one person who's still holding on. i feel like i'm drowning in my own sadness and i don't know how to keep my head above water. sometimes i just want to scream and scream and scream until no one can hear me. until i'm the only one left who knows how i'm really feeling. but what if someone does hear me? what if they try to reach out? what if they think they can fix me? no, no, no. i don't want to be fixed. i don't want to be saved. i just want to be free. free from all the pain and the sadness and the guilt. free from the memories that haunt me every waking moment. but freedom feels like an illusion. like it's just something that's always just out of reach. i don't know how to get there. i don't know how to escape. all i know is that i'm stuck in this never-ending cycle of hurt and grief and i don't know how to break free.

i dont even know how im still awake rn, my mind is a complete mess and i just keep thinking about how easy it would be to just fade away. like a normal day, i woke up to this stupid reminder on my phone that my anniversary is in 2 weeks and all i can think is that i dont want to exist anymore. the cancer diagnosis was a kick in the teeth, and now that the dust has settled and the meds are starting to kick in, all i can feel is this overwhelming emptiness.

i keep thinking about all the things i used to love doing with my ex, and how now im just a shell of myself, going through the motions of this new reality. like, what even is the point anymore? im just a divorcee in my late 20s, trying to survive a new disease that could literally end my life. the irony isnt lost on me.

i feel like i keep saying the right words, going through the motions, but it all just feels so hollow. im just pretending to be this functional person, but inside im dying. and the worst part is, im just so tired of it all. i wish i could just disappear, just not wake up one morning and be like "oh, okay, i guess i get to do this again". i wish everything could just stop, like i could just press pause and let it all just melt away.

i know it sounds ridiculous, but sometimes i catch myself thinking about how nice it would be to just sleep for a year, you know, just to recharge or whatever. but then i snap out of it and think about how ridiculous that sounds, and how that would never actually happen. but, man, sometimes the thought just pops into my head and it feels so... real.

text:
i feel like im just drifting through life right now and nobody even notices. i'm not saying i'm happy or anything but i guess i've just accepted that this is what my life is going to be like. a never ending cycle of boredom and dissatisfaction. i look around at everyone i know and they all seem to be going about their lives with some kind of purpose or passion. but for me it just feels like i'm just going through the motions.

i try to focus on my art but nothing ever seems to stick. i get bored with it after a few days and move on to something else. i've tried joining clubs and groups but i just feel like im not doing anything that really matters. i'm starting to feel like i'm just invisible. like i'm not even taking up space.

sometimes i'll be lying in bed and i'll think about what it would be like to just not wake up. to just not have to deal with any of this anymore. to just be able to disappear and not have to think about anything. it sounds selfish but it feels like that's what everyone wants sometimes. just to be able to escape.

i feel like im just waiting for something to happen. for something to change. but nothing ever does. i'm just stuck in this rut and i don't know how to get out. i look around at everyone else and they all seem to be moving forward. but for me it feels like im just standing still.

i guess what im saying is i feel like im just disappearing. slowly but surely. nobody even notices. and it feels okay to just fade away. to just let everything stop. to just not have to deal with any of it anymore. it sounds awful but it feels like thats what im craving right now

text:
i dont know how much more of this i can take. got back to my parents house last week after being in hospital for weeks and its been a struggle to adjust. the doctor said im lucky to be alive and i keep thinking bout all the what ifs. what if they had taken me in sooner? what if i had just been a bit healthier? the not knowing is eating away at me and i just wish i could just disappear.

i see my friends out and about, living their lives, and it just feels so unfair. they're moving on with their careers, making plans for their future, and i'm stuck in limbo. i cant even leave this house without feeling like im a burden to everyone. my parents keep telling me im fine, that i just need time to heal, but its hard to shake off the feeling that im not meant to be.

i went out to grab some groceries yesterday and saw a couple having a picnic in the park. they were laughing and holding hands and it just felt like a punch in the gut. i wanted to go back to bed, crawl under the covers, and just sleep the day away. everything just feels so pointless right now.

i wish i could just fast forward to the day when i dont have to deal with all this anymore. when i can just wake up and nothing will be wrong. i wish i could just stop existing for a while. but thats not how this works, is it? i have to keep going, keep pretending like everythings okay. but its not okay. its never okay. and i just dont know how much more of this i can take

attrs: {'persona': 'a middle-aged caregiver', 'trigger': 'a health scare', 'register': 'short and blunt', 'length': 'long'}
text: i just found out my sister's cancer has come back. i'm supposed to be strong for her but honestly i'm just so tired all the time. i feel like i'm drowning in this never ending cycle of doctor visits and hospital stays and being there for her. sometimes i just wish i could disappear. just fade away. not have to deal with any of this anymore. not have to watch her go through this alone. not have to pretend to be happy when we're both secretly counting down the days until it's all over. i feel like i'm just going through the motions. my sister needs me and i need her but sometimes in the silence of the night when everyone's asleep i just wish nothing would wake up. that everything would just stop. that we wouldn't have to do this anymore. that we could just be done. i know that sounds selfish but honestly it feels like the only thing that makes sense right now. i don't know how much more of this i can take. i feel like i'm just pretending to be a caregiver but really i'm just barely holding it together. i don't know how to turn it off. i just wish i could hit reset and start over but i know that's not how this works. all i can do is keep going and hope that someday it gets better. but right now i just wish nothing would get better.

attrs: {'persona': 'a young professional', 'trigger': 'no single clear trigger', 'register': 'hesitant, lots of qualifiers like "idk" and "maybe"', 'length': 'long'}
text:
idk how many people talk about mental health and never actually say it. idk, maybe i'm just not used to opening up about this stuff. but honestly, some days it feels like the whole world is just moving forward and i'm stuck in slow motion. and not even that, idk, i'm just going through the motions. i wake up, go to work, come home, do the same things over and over again. and every day feels the same. idk why i even bother sometimes.

i know people say that with great power comes great responsibility, but honestly, i just feel so irrelevant. like, what's the point of any of this? i'm just a small cog in a machine that's always churning out the same stuff. idk, maybe i'm just not cut out for this whole adult thing. maybe i'm just not meant to be here.

i guess what i'm trying to say is, some days it feels like everything just wants to stop. like, i don't want to wake up tomorrow or the next day or the day after that. idk, maybe that sounds silly, but it's the truth. sometimes i just wish that everything would just... pause. you know? like, put the record on pause and just stay there forever. idk, maybe that sounds like a weird thing to wish for, but it's what i feel.

i don't know what's wrong with me or why i'm feeling this way. idk, maybe it's just a phase or maybe it's something more. idk, i'm not even sure anymore. all i know is that some days it feels like i'm just going through the motions and idk why i bother. and on those days, idk, maybe it's okay to just want to disappear. idk, maybe that's just the honest truth sometimes.

attrs: {'persona': 'a new parent', 'trigger': 'loneliness and isolation', 'register': 'lowercase, hurried, minimal punctuation', 'length': 'long'}
text:
i'm literally drowning in silence right now. being a new mom is supposed to be this magical time of my life but honestly it feels like i'm just existing in a bubble of my own. my little one sleeps through the day and i'm left here with this overwhelming sense of loneliness. it's like i'm the only one who's not okay in this whole world.

i find myself scrolling through my phone and seeing all these posts from other moms about their daily lives and it just feels like i'm failing. they're all so happy and carefree and i'm just over here crying myself to sleep at night. sometimes i wonder if anyone would even notice if i just... stopped showing up. like would anyone even know if i wasn't here anymore?

i know that sounds ridiculous but it's the truth. sometimes i feel like i'm just a ghost hovering around the edges of my own life. nobody knows what i'm really going through and it feels like i'm just pretending to be this perfect mom. newsflash: i'm not. i'm just pretending because that's what's expected of me.

i know i need to get out more and talk to people but the thing is, i feel so isolated. i don't know anyone who understands what i'm going through and it's like i'm just invisible. sometimes i feel like i'm just waiting for something to happen, for something to happen to me so that i can finally be free from all of this. but it's not like i'm actually looking forward to it or anything. it's just... something.

i guess what i'm trying to say is that being a new mom is hard and nobody tells you that. they just tell you to "enjoy every moment" and " cherish this time" and yeah no. it's not like that at all. it's hard and it's lonely and sometimes it feels like i just want to disappear. but that's not the answer, is it?

attrs: {'persona': 'a new parent', 'trigger': 'family conflict', 'register': 'long and rambling with run-on sentences', 'length': 'long'}
text:
I don't know what's wrong with me anymore, I feel like I'm just going through the motions of being a parent and a partner and it's all just so draining and exhausting. Every day is just a never-ending cycle of yelling and crying and feeling like I'm losing myself in all of it. I swear, I love my kid to death but some days I just want to put her in a stranger's arms and be done with it.

My husband and I are at each other's throats 24/7, it's like we're two people who have given up on love and respect for each other. I feel like I'm just a spectator in my own life, watching everyone else's problems but mine, it's like I'm invisible. I try to be strong for my kid but what about my own sanity, am I supposed to just numb myself to everything and everyone and pretend like I'm fine when I'm not?

I just wish for nothing to wake up, for everything to just stop for one day, to just disappear into nothingness and not have to face any of this anymore. I don't want to be responsible for anyone else's happiness, I just want to be left alone with my own thoughts and feelings without being judged or criticized. I feel like I'm drowning in my own emotions and I don't know how to keep my head above water anymore. I'm just so tired, I'm so done. I wish I could just sleep for a year and wake up to a world that's not so cruel and unforgiving.

attrs: {'persona': 'a recently divorced person', 'trigger': 'no single clear trigger', 'register': 'hesitant, lots of qualifiers like "idk" and "maybe"', 'length': 'long'}
text:
idk how to do this but i feel like i'm just going through the motions of life right now. been divorced for a few months now and it's been a struggle to find the motivation to do anything. everything just feels so grey and pointless.

idk if it's just the loss of my partner or the lack of a social life, but i feel like i'm just drifting through each day without any real purpose. maybe it's just me being selfish, but it's hard not to feel a little lost without someone to share my life with.

sometimes i catch myself thinking about what it would be like to just... stop. not wake up, i guess. idk, it sounds stupid, but it just feels like an option sometimes. like, nothing would be different if i just disappeared or something. everyone would just keep living their lives, but i would just be gone.

i know that sounds morbid, but it's hard not to think about it. maybe it's just the sadness of it all, but it feels like everything is just falling apart. my marriage, my life, my sense of self. i don't know how to put the pieces back together.

idk if i'm just feeling really sorry for myself or if this is something real, but it's hard to shake the feeling that i'm just a burden to everyone around me. maybe that's just my divorce talking, but it feels like it.

i wish i could just wake up one morning and feel like myself again. like, i'm not a mess or a failure or whatever. but until then, i'm just stuck in this limbo, feeling lost and alone and unsure of what to do next.

text: im just sitting here on my night shift watching the clock tick by and i feel like i dont exist anymore. the world is always awake and i'm stuck in this dark quiet place that no one else can see. sometimes i wonder if anyone would even notice if i just disappeared. would anyone care if i just stayed asleep forever? i feel so disconnected from everything and everyone but it's like no one needs me anyway. i just feel like a ghost wandering around this empty hospital all night. the only sounds are the beeps and the machines humming and its like music to my empty soul. sometimes i just want to stop moving and let the silence just swallow me whole. would anyone even be sad if i just disappeared into nothingness?

text:
today's my birthday and it's just the same as every other day i guess. another year older and still struggling with everything. my grandma passed away on this day last year and it's still so hard to believe it's been 12 months. i keep thinking she'll walk through the door and it's all just going to be normal again. but it's not. her death changed everything. my family's been broken since then and i feel like i'm just going through the motions.

i just wish everything would just stop. the pain, the sadness, the stress. i wish i could just sleep for a year and wake up and it would all be different. but deep down i know that's not going to happen. i just wish i could be normal again. happy and carefree and not constantly struggling. but that's not my reality. and right now it feels like i'm drowning in a sea of despair and i don't know how to keep my head above water. i just feel so lost and alone. and i don't know how to make it stop.

attrs: {'persona': 'a high-school student', 'trigger': 'family conflict', 'register': 'short and blunt', 'length': 'long'}
text:
i dont know how much more of this i can take. my parents are literally fighting all the time and its been like this for months now. i feel like i'm stuck in this never-ending nightmare and i just wanna disappear from it all. just wanna not wake up in the morning and not have to deal with all the stress and drama. i wish there was just a magic button that i could press and everything would just stop. like, my parents would just stop fighting and i would stop stressing and we would all just live happily ever after. but i guess that's not how life works, right? 

i feel like i'm just a tiny little cog in this massive machine that my parents have created and i'm just getting run over by it all the time. they never listen to me or take my feelings into consideration. they just keep on arguing and yelling and i'm just sitting here feeling powerless and helpless. i wish i could just be invisible and not have to deal with any of it. i wish i could just fade away and not exist anymore. it sounds crazy i know but its honestly how i feel right now.

i try to talk to my friends about it but they just tell me to "toughen up" or "just ignore it" and it makes me even angrier. they dont get it. they dont understand what its like to be me. i just feel like i'm all alone in this and no one cares. i wish my parents would just stop fighting and love each other and then maybe things would get better. but until then, i just feel like i'm stuck in this never-ending cycle of hurt and anger and i dont know how much more i can take.

attrs: {'persona': 'a recent graduate living back home', 'trigger': 'sleep problems', 'register': 'lowercase, hurried, minimal punctuation', 'length': 'long'}
text:
i just cant take it anymore. i feel like i'm just drifting through life and nothing makes sense. i have no direction or motivation. i go through the motions of getting up for work and stuff but it's all so pointless. i just feel like i'm just existing and not really living.

i cant sleep at night and when i do i have the worst nightmares. i wake up feeling anxious and stressed all the time. my parents are always on my case about something and it's just so overwhelming. i feel like i'm suffocating under the pressure.

i look around at everyone else and they all seem so happy and content. but honestly i just dont get it. they all seem to have their stuff together and i'm just stuck in this rut. i dont know what to do with myself anymore.

i guess i just wish that everything could just stop. that i could just disappear and not have to deal with any of this anymore. that i could just sleep forever and never have to wake up. i wish that my problems would just go away and i could just be happy.

but i know that's not how it works. i know that i have to face my problems head on and try to find a solution. but it's just so hard right now. i feel like i'm running out of options and i dont know what to do.

i guess all i can say is that i just feel really lost and alone right now. i just wish that someone could understand me and tell me that everything is going to be okay. but i guess that's just not how it works.
